name: Gestational Cholestasis
text: Itchy skin, particularly on the hands and feet

name: Injury to Internal Organ
text: Abdominal pain, bleeding, organ dysfunction

name: Injury to Internal Organ
text: Immediate medical attention, diagnostic tests, surgery (in some cases)

name: Scabies
text: Intense itching, especially at night, small blisters or bumps

name: Scabies
text: Prescription medications (topical or oral scabicides), washing clothes and bedding in hot water, vacuuming and cleaning home

name: Congenital Glaucoma
text: Cloudy or hazy eyes, excessive tearing, sensitivity to light

name: Congenital Glaucoma
text: Surgery (e.g., trabeculotomy, goniotomy) to create a new drainage pathway, medications to reduce intraocular pressure

name: Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder (ARFID)
text: Avoidance or restriction of certain foods or entire food groups, significant weight loss or failure to gain weight

name: Avoidant/Restrictive Food Intake Disorder (ARFID)
text: Nutritional counseling, psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or family-based therapy), medical monitoring, exposure therapy, occupational therapy

name: Subdural hemorrhage
text: Headache, confusion, dizziness, nausea or vomiting, seizures, weakness or numbness

name: Subdural hemorrhage
text: Immediate medical attention, close monitoring of vital signs and neurological status, diagnostic imaging (such as CT or MRI scan), potential surgical intervention (such as craniotomy or burr hole evacuation), medication to manage symptoms

name: Poisoning due to Antidepressants
text: Nausea, vomiting, drowsiness, dizziness, confusion, seizures, changes in heart rate, low blood pressure, respiratory distress

name: Poisoning due to Antidepressants
text: Seek immediate medical assistance or contact a poison control center, activated charcoal (if indicated), supportive care (maintaining vital signs, airway management), gastric lavage (in certain cases), administration of antidotes (if available and appropriate), monitoring and management of complications

name: Mittelschmerz
text: Lower abdominal pain and discomfort occurring around the time of ovulation

name: Mittelschmerz
text: Pain management (over-the-counter pain relievers, heat therapy), hormonal birth control (if severe or recurrent)

name: Cornea Infection
text: Eye redness, pain or discomfort, blurred or decreased vision, increased sensitivity to light

name: Cornea Infection
text: Antibiotic or antifungal eye drops, oral medications, supportive care, possible corneal transplant (in severe cases)

name: Myopia
text: Blurred distance vision, eyestrain, squinting

name: Myopia
text: Eyeglasses, contact lenses, refractive surgery (LASIK), orthokeratology (for some cases)

name: Abscess of the Lung
text: Cough with phlegm or pus, chest pain, fever, shortness of breath, fatigue

name: Abscess of the Lung
text: Antibiotics, drainage or surgical removal of the abscess, supportive care, management of underlying lung conditions

name: Congenital rubella
text: Developmental delays, intellectual disability, hearing loss, vision problems, heart defects, liver or spleen enlargement, rash at birth

name: Congenital rubella
text: Supportive care to manage symptoms and complications, early intervention services for developmental delays, educational support, vision and hearing interventions, specialized medical care as needed

name: Intertrigo
text: Red, raw, or inflamed skin in skin folds (such as armpits or groin), itching or burning sensation

name: Intertrigo
text: Keeping the affected area clean and dry, applying topical antifungal or antibacterial creams, using barrier creams

name: Insect Bites
text: Red, swollen, itchy bumps

name: Insect Bites
text: Topical corticosteroids, oral antihistamines, cold compresses

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome
text: Severe and chronic pain, often in the limbs, with associated swelling, changes in skin color or temperature, abnormal sweating, limited range of motion

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome
text: Physical therapy (including exercises, stretching, and mirror therapy), pain management techniques (such as medications, nerve blocks, or spinal cord stimulation), sympathetic nerve blocks, psychological support (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or counseling), occupational therapy, graded motor imagery, transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS), patient education and self-management techniques

name: Anxiety
text: Excessive worrying, restlessness, increased heart rate, panic attacks

name: Anxiety
text: Therapy (cognitive-behavioral therapy, psychotherapy), medications (antidepressants, anti-anxiety drugs)

name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
text: Heartburn, acid reflux, chest pain

name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
text: Lifestyle changes, medications (antacids, proton pump inhibitors)

name: Crushing Injury
text: Severe pain, swelling, deformity, bruising, difficulty moving or using the affected area

name: Crushing Injury
text: Emergency medical attention, immobilization, pain management, surgery (to repair or reconstruct affected tissues), rehabilitation (physical therapy, occupational therapy)

name: Cellulitis
text: Redness, Pain, tenderness, Swelling, Skin changes, Lymph node enlargement

name: Cellulitis
text: Antibiotics, Warm compresses, immobilization, rest, Pain management

name: Anal Fissure
text: Pain during bowel movements, rectal bleeding

name: Anal Fissure
text: Stool softeners, fiber supplements, topical ointments, surgical procedures (in severe cases)

name: Neuropathic Pain
text: Shooting or burning pain, tingling or numbness

name: Neuropathic Pain
text: Medications (such as antidepressants, anticonvulsants, topical treatments), nerve blocks, spinal cord stimulation, physical therapy, occupational therapy, transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS), acupuncture, cognitive-behavioral therapy, relaxation techniques

name: Common Cold
text: cough, sore throat, runny nose, sneezing

name: Common Cold
text: rest, fluids, over-the-counter medication

name: Food Allergy
text: Rash, itching, swelling, difficulty breathing, gastrointestinal symptoms

name: Food Allergy
text: Avoidance of allergenic food, antihistamines, epinephrine (in severe cases)

name: Pulmonary Fibrosis
text: Shortness of breath, dry cough, fatigue

name: Pulmonary Fibrosis
text: Medications (immunosuppressants, antifibrotic drugs), oxygen therapy, lung transplantation

name: Hyperosmotic Hyperketotic State
text: Increased blood sugar levels, increased ketone production, excessive thirst, frequent urination, fatigue, confusion, fruity breath odor

name: Hyperosmotic Hyperketotic State
text: Insulin therapy to regulate blood sugar levels, intravenous fluids to correct dehydration, electrolyte replacement, correction of underlying causes (e.g., diabetes management)

name: Overflow Incontinence
text: Frequent or constant dribbling of urine, inability to completely empty the bladder, weak urine stream

name: Overflow Incontinence
text: Bladder training exercises, double voiding, catheterization, medications (alpha-blockers, anticholinergics), surgery (in severe cases), lifestyle modifications

name: Abdominal Injury
text: Abdominal pain, tenderness, bruising, swelling, difficulty breathing, nausea, vomiting, changes in bowel movements, blood in urine or stool

name: Abdominal Injury
text: Immediate medical attention, X-ray, CT scan, ultrasound, stabilization of vital signs, pain management, observation for signs of internal bleeding, potential surgical intervention, wound care

name: Hirsutism
text: Excessive hair growth in women

name: Hirsutism
text: Medications (anti-androgens, oral contraceptives), hair removal methods, lifestyle changes

name: Neuralgia
text: Sharp, shooting pain along the affected nerve

name: Neuralgia
text: Medications (pain relievers, anticonvulsants), nerve blocks

name: Warthin tumor
text: Painless lump or swelling, Facial changes

name: Warthin tumor
text: Surgical excision

name: Presbycusis
text: Gradual hearing loss, difficulty hearing high-pitched sounds, speech sounds muffled or unclear

name: Presbycusis
text: Hearing aids, assistive listening devices, cochlear implants (in severe cases), communication strategies, counseling, regular hearing evaluations

name: Opioid Use Disorder
text: Compulsive use of opioids, strong desire or cravings for opioids, inability to control or reduce opioid use, withdrawal symptoms (such as nausea, muscle aches, anxiety) when attempting to stop or reduce use, tolerance to opioids, continued use despite negative consequences (such as legal issues, health problems)

name: Opioid Use Disorder
text: Medication-assisted treatment (such as methadone, buprenorphine, naltrexone), counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, contingency management), support groups (such as Narcotics Anonymous), harm reduction strategies, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Panic disorder
text: Palpitations, Sweating, Trembling, Shortness of breath, Fear of losing control, Dizziness

name: Panic disorder
text: Antidepressant medications, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Relaxation Techniques

name: Esophageal Cancer
text: Difficulty swallowing, unintentional weight loss, chest pain, hoarseness

name: Esophageal Cancer
text: Surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, palliative care, clinical trials

name: Alcohol Intoxication
text: Slurred speech, impaired coordination, confusion

name: Alcohol Intoxication
text: Supportive care, ensuring hydration, monitoring vital signs, prevention of complications

name: Pilonidal Cyst
text: Pain or discomfort in the tailbone area, swelling, redness, drainage of pus or blood, abscess formation

name: Pilonidal Cyst
text: Warm compresses, pain management, antibiotics (for infection), incision and drainage, surgical removal or excision, wound care, lifestyle modifications to prevent recurrence

name: Gestational Diabetes
text: High blood sugar levels during pregnancy

name: Gestational Diabetes
text: Blood sugar monitoring, healthy diet modifications, regular exercise, insulin injections (if needed), close monitoring by healthcare provider

name: Drug Withdrawal
text: Anxiety, restlessness, irritability, tremors, sweating, nausea, vomiting, insomnia, drug cravings

name: Drug Withdrawal
text: Gradual tapering of the drug under medical supervision, medications to manage withdrawal symptoms (such as benzodiazepines, clonidine, or buprenorphine), behavioral therapy, support groups, counseling, rehabilitation programs

name: Fetal Alcohol Syndrome
text: Growth deficiencies, facial abnormalities, developmental delays, intellectual disability

name: Fetal Alcohol Syndrome
text: Early intervention services for developmental delays, educational support, speech and occupational therapy, behavioral interventions, medical management of associated health issues

name: Polycythemia Vera
text: Increased red blood cell count, fatigue, headache, dizziness, itching, enlarged spleen

name: Polycythemia Vera
text: Phlebotomy (removal of blood), medication (hydroxyurea, interferon), low-dose aspirin, managing complications (blood thinners, medication for itching)

name: Osteoarthritis
text: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling

name: Osteoarthritis
text: Pain relievers, physical therapy, weight management, assistive devices, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Fracture of the Neck
text: Severe neck pain, swelling, tenderness, difficulty moving or turning the head, numbness or weakness in the arms or legs

name: Fracture of the Neck
text: Immobilization (cervical collar, brace), pain management, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases)

name: Myoclonus
text: Sudden, brief muscle contractions or jerks, often affecting the arms, legs, or face

name: Myoclonus
text: Identifying and treating underlying causes (e.g., medication adjustments, metabolic imbalances), medications (anti-seizure drugs, tranquilizers), physical therapy, supportive care

name: Foreign Body in the Gastrointestinal Tract
text: Abdominal pain or discomfort, difficulty swallowing, choking or gagging, drooling, nausea or vomiting, loss of appetite, chest pain or pressure, respiratory distress, coughing or wheezing, gastrointestinal bleeding, bowel obstruction, perforation of the digestive tract

name: Foreign Body in the Gastrointestinal Tract
text: Observation and monitoring (for small, smooth, and non-sharp objects that are likely to pass through the digestive tract without complications), endoscopic removal (using a flexible tube with a camera to locate and remove the foreign body), surgical intervention (in cases of severe complications, large or sharp objects, or failed endoscopic removal attempts), supportive care (such as intravenous fluids, pain management, and antibiotics if infection is present), imaging studies (such as X-rays or CT scans) to evaluate the location and extent of the foreign body, consultation with a gastroenterologist or surgeon for appropriate management

name: Coronary Atherosclerosis
text: Chest pain or discomfort (angina), shortness of breath, fatigue, irregular heartbeat, dizziness, nausea, sweating, jaw or arm pain (in some cases), heart attack (when a coronary artery becomes completely blocked)

name: Coronary Atherosclerosis
text: Lifestyle changes (such as a heart-healthy diet, regular exercise, weight management, smoking cessation), medication (such as aspirin, cholesterol-lowering medications, beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, nitroglycerin), procedures (such as angioplasty and stenting, coronary artery bypass grafting), cardiac rehabilitation, management of other risk factors (such as high blood pressure, diabetes)

name: Hypertensive Heart Disease
text: High blood pressure, chest pain or discomfort, shortness of breath, fatigue

name: Hypertensive Heart Disease
text: Medications (antihypertensives), lifestyle changes (healthy diet, exercise, stress management), treatment of underlying conditions

name: Mumps
text: Swollen salivary glands (usually parotid), fever, headache, fatigue

name: Mumps
text: Supportive care, pain relievers, isolation, vaccination (MMR)

name: Cholesteatoma
text: Ear pain, hearing loss, ear drainage, dizziness or vertigo, tinnitus

name: Cholesteatoma
text: Surgical removal, antibiotics (for associated infections)

name: Polycystic Kidney Disease
text: Abdominal pain, high blood pressure, kidney cysts

name: Polycystic Kidney Disease
text: Symptom management, blood pressure control, dialysis

name: Juvenile Rheumatoid Arthritis
text: Joint pain, swelling, stiffness, limited range of motion, fever, rash

name: Juvenile Rheumatoid Arthritis
text: Medications (NSAIDs, DMARDs), physical therapy, occupational therapy, exercise, assistive devices

name: Hypospadias
text: Abnormal positioning of the urethral opening on the underside of the penis, penile curvature, spraying of urine, difficulty in urination

name: Hypospadias
text: Surgical repair (hypospadias repair surgery), hormone therapy (in some cases), counseling and support for the child and family

name: Parotid stones
text: Swelling, pain, dry mouth, bad taste

name: Parotid stones
text: Hydration,Saliva stimulation, Stone removal

name: Achalasia
text: Difficulty swallowing

name: Achalasia
text: Balloon dilation, surgery, medication

name: Rumination Disorder
text: Repeated regurgitation and re-chewing of food

name: Rumination Disorder
text: Behavioral therapy (such as diaphragmatic breathing techniques), biofeedback, addressing any underlying psychological or medical conditions, dietary modifications

name: Bronchitis
text: Persistent cough with yellow or green mucus, chest congestion, fatigue

name: Bronchitis
text: Rest, fluids, over-the-counter cough suppressants, inhalers (if prescribed), treating underlying cause

name: Balanitis
text: Inflammation of the glans penis (tip of the penis), redness, swelling, pain

name: Balanitis
text: Good hygiene, topical antifungal or antibacterial creams, corticosteroid creams (if severe), treating underlying causes

name: Ectopic Pregnancy
text: Vaginal bleeding, abdominal pain or cramping, shoulder pain (if the fallopian tube ruptures)

name: Ectopic Pregnancy
text: Immediate medical attention, potential medical procedures (such as medication or surgery to remove the ectopic pregnancy), close monitoring, emotional support

name: Atelectasis
text: Difficulty breathing, rapid breathing, chest pain

name: Atelectasis
text: Treatment of the underlying cause (such as removal of airway obstruction, treatment of lung infection), deep breathing exercises, coughing techniques, incentive spirometry, chest physiotherapy, bronchodilator medications, supplemental oxygen therapy

name: Anorexia Nervosa
text: Extreme weight loss, fear of gaining weight, distorted body image

name: Anorexia Nervosa
text: Medical monitoring, nutritional counseling, psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or family-based therapy), medications (in some cases), hospitalization (for severe cases), support groups

name: Hypergammaglobulinemia
text: Elevated levels of gamma globulins in the blood, may be asymptomatic or associated with other underlying conditions

name: Hypergammaglobulinemia
text: Treatment of underlying cause or associated condition, immunosuppressive therapy (in some cases)

name: Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension
text: High blood pressure during pregnancy

name: Pregnancy-Induced Hypertension
text: Monitoring blood pressure, lifestyle modifications (such as salt restriction, regular exercise), medication (such as antihypertensive drugs) if necessary, close monitoring by healthcare provider

name: Chorioretinitis
text: Eye pain, blurred vision, sensitivity to light

name: Chorioretinitis
text: Antiviral medications, corticosteroids, eye drops

name: Open-Angle Glaucoma
text: Gradual loss of peripheral vision, tunnel vision

name: Open-Angle Glaucoma
text: Eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (e.g., prostaglandin analogs, beta blockers, alpha agonists, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), oral medications (e.g., carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), laser trabeculoplasty, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Cranial Nerve Palsy
text: Depends on the specific cranial nerve involved; symptoms can include vision changes, eye movement abnormalities, facial weakness or paralysis, difficulty swallowing or speaking, loss of sensation in the face or other areas

name: Cranial Nerve Palsy
text: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and specific cranial nerve affected. It may include medications (such as corticosteroids or antiviral drugs), physical therapy, speech therapy, occupational therapy, eye exercises, surgery (in some cases), and management of any underlying conditions.

name: Corneal Abrasion
text: Eye pain, redness, foreign body sensation, sensitivity to light

name: Corneal Abrasion
text: Eye patching, lubricating eye drops, antibiotic ointments (if infected), avoiding further eye irritation

name: Hiatal Hernia
text: Heartburn, chest pain, difficulty swallowing

name: Hiatal Hernia
text: Lifestyle changes, medications (antacids, proton pump inhibitors)

name: Type 2 Diabetes
text: increased thirst, frequent urination, fatigue

name: Type 2 Diabetes
text: insulin, medication, lifestyle change

name: Uterine Fibroids
text: Heavy or prolonged menstrual periods, pelvic pain or pressure, frequent urination, difficulty emptying the bladder, constipation, backache, leg pain, enlarged abdomen or uterus, infertility or recurrent miscarriages (in some cases)

name: Uterine Fibroids
text: Watchful waiting (for small or asymptomatic fibroids), medication (such as hormonal birth control, GnRH agonists, progestin-releasing intrauterine devices), minimally invasive procedures (such as uterine artery embolization, myomectomy), hysteroscopic procedures (such as hysteroscopic myomectomy or endometrial ablation), surgical removal of fibroids (such as laparoscopic or robotic-assisted myomectomy, hysterectomy), focused ultrasound ablation, hormonal therapy, alternative approaches (such as acupuncture or herbal remedies)

name: Angle-Closure Glaucoma
text: Severe eye pain, headache, blurred vision, halos around lights, nausea and vomiting

name: Angle-Closure Glaucoma
text: Medications (e.g., oral or intravenous acetazolamide, topical beta blockers, alpha agonists, prostaglandin analogs), laser peripheral iridotomy, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Chronic Headache
text: Persistent head pain, tension in neck and shoulders

name: Chronic Headache
text: Pain-relieving medications, relaxation techniques, stress management

name: Acute Kidney Injury
text: Decreased urine output, fluid retention, fatigue

name: Acute Kidney Injury
text: Treating underlying cause, supportive care, medications to manage complications, dialysis (in severe cases)

name: Scarlet Fever
text: Sore throat, fever, rash (red, sandpaper-like), swollen glands

name: Scarlet Fever
text: Antibiotics (such as penicillin or erythromycin), rest, fluids, symptomatic relief

name: Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm
text: Chest or back pain, shortness of breath, hoarseness, difficulty swallowing

name: Thoracic Aortic Aneurysm
text: Regular monitoring and imaging tests to assess aneurysm size and growth, lifestyle modifications (e.g., blood pressure control, avoiding smoking, managing cholesterol levels), medication (e.g., beta-blockers, ACE inhibitors) to manage blood pressure and reduce the risk of rupture, surgical intervention

name: Scleroderma
text: Thickening of the skin, Raynaud's phenomenon

name: Scleroderma
text: Medications (immunosuppressants), therapy, symptom management

name: Bone Spur of the Calcaneus
text: Heel pain, especially when walking or standing, tenderness, swelling, inflammation of the surrounding tissue

name: Bone Spur of the Calcaneus
text: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), orthotic devices, physical therapy, stretching exercises, shoe modifications, corticosteroid injections, surgical removal (in severe cases)

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-2
text: Metabolic acidosis, apid breathing, rapid heart rate, confusion, headache, dizziness, seizures

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-2
text: Blood tests, Supportive Measures, Gastric Decontamination, Antidote Administration, Hemodialysis

name: Pica
text: Persistent consumption of non-food substances

name: Pica
text: Behavioral therapy, addressing underlying nutritional deficiencies, addressing any medical complications, family support

name: Induced Abortion
text: Vaginal bleeding, cramping, abdominal pain, nausea, fatigue, emotional distress

name: Induced Abortion
text: Medical Abortion: Medication (such as mifepristone and misoprostol) is used to induce abortion, which can be done in the early stages of pregnancy. Surgical Abortion: A healthcare professional performs a procedure to remove the pregnancy, which can involve suction aspiration or dilation and curettage (D&C). It's important to consult with a healthcare professional to discuss the options and determine the most suitable method.

name: Tension Headache
text: Mild to moderate head pain, tightness or pressure sensation on both sides of head

name: Tension Headache
text: Over-the-counter pain relievers, relaxation techniques, stress management

name: Tuberculosis
text: cough, weight loss, fever, night sweats

name: Tuberculosis
text: antibiotics, long-term medication

name: Urethral Valves
text: Difficulty or poor urinary stream, urinary tract infections, urinary frequency or urgency, abdominal distension, poor weight gain

name: Urethral Valves
text: Surgical intervention (valve ablation or resection), catheterization, medications for symptoms and complications

name: Hashimoto Thyroiditis
text: Fatigue, weight gain, constipation, dry skin, depression, muscle aches and stiffness, sensitivity to cold

name: Hashimoto Thyroiditis
text: Thyroid hormone replacement (levothyroxine), monitoring and management of symptoms

name: Parkinson Disease
text: Tremors, stiffness, slowness of movement

name: Parkinson Disease
text: Medications (levodopa, dopamine agonists), physical therapy, deep brain stimulation

name: Cryptorchidism
text: Absence or undescended testicle(s), empty scrotum, smaller or underdeveloped testicle(s), inguinal hernia, abnormal positioning of the testicle(s) (higher in the groin area)

name: Cryptorchidism
text: Observation and monitoring (in cases of mild or transient cryptorchidism), hormone therapy (to stimulate testicular descent), surgical intervention (orchiopexy) to reposition the testicle(s) into the scrotum, if necessary, performed around the age of 6 to 12 months, open or laparoscopic surgery (in cases of high or persistent cryptorchidism), testicular prostheses (in cases of absent or non-functional testicles), regular follow-up visits with a urologist or pediatric surgeon

name: Mucocele
text: Painless fluid-filled swelling in the oral cavity

name: Mucocele
text: Observation, Surgical removal if necessary

name: Empyema
text: Fever, chest pain, productive cough with foul-smelling or bloody sputum

name: Empyema
text: Antibiotics, drainage (chest tube insertion, thoracentesis), surgical intervention (decortication), supportive care, treatment of underlying cause

name: Abdominal Hernia
text: Visible bulge or swelling, discomfort or pain

name: Abdominal Hernia
text: Watchful waiting, hernia truss, surgical repair, lifestyle changes

name: Hemarthrosis
text: Joint swelling, pain, warmth, limited range of motion

name: Hemarthrosis
text: RICE method (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), pain management, joint aspiration or drainage (in severe cases)

name: Pregnancy-Related Back Pain
text: Lower back pain, discomfort in the pelvic area

name: Pregnancy-Related Back Pain
text: Prenatal exercises (such as stretching and strengthening exercises), good posture, heat or cold therapy, prenatal massages, supportive devices (such as pregnancy belts), physical therapy

name: Muscle Spasm
text: Sudden, involuntary muscle contractions, muscle pain, stiffness, limited range of motion

name: Muscle Spasm
text: Stretching and relaxation exercises, heat or cold therapy, over-the-counter pain relievers, muscle relaxants, physical therapy, lifestyle modifications

name: Diverticulosis
text: Abdominal pain, bloating, changes in bowel movements

name: Diverticulosis
text: High-fiber diet, medications (pain relievers, antibiotics)

name: Dry Skin
text: Dry, flaky skin with itching

name: Dry Skin
text: Moisturizing creams or lotions, avoiding hot showers or baths, using mild soaps

name: Alcohol Use Disorder
text: Cravings for alcohol, inability to control or limit alcohol consumption, withdrawal symptoms (such as tremors, anxiety, sweating) when attempting to stop or reduce alcohol use, continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems, health issues), neglecting responsibilities, tolerance to alcohol

name: Alcohol Use Disorder
text: Detoxification (if necessary), counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing), support groups (such as Alcoholics Anonymous), medication (such as disulfiram, naltrexone, acamprosate), addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Muscular Dystrophy
text: Progressive muscle weakness and degeneration, difficulty walking or moving, muscle wasting

name: Muscular Dystrophy
text: Supportive care, physical therapy, assistive devices, medications for symptom management, gene therapies (in some cases)

name: Vaginal Yeast Infection
text: Itching and irritation in the vagina and vulva, redness and swelling of the vulva, white, thick, clumpy vaginal discharge (resembling cottage cheese), burning sensation during urination, pain during sexual intercourse, rash or soreness in the affected area

name: Vaginal Yeast Infection
text: Over-the-counter antifungal creams, ointments, or suppositories (such as miconazole, clotrimazole, or terconazole), prescription-strength antifungal medications (in severe or recurrent cases), oral antifungal medications (if recommended by a healthcare professional), topical corticosteroids (for relief of itching and inflammation), maintaining good hygiene practices, avoiding irritants (such as scented products), wearing breathable cotton underwear, avoiding tight-fitting clothing, probiotics (to restore healthy vaginal flora)

name: Osteoporosis
text: Fragile bones, loss of height over time, back pain, fractures

name: Osteoporosis
text: Calcium and vitamin D supplements, regular exercise, medications to slow bone loss

name: Syndrome of Inappropriate Secretion of ADH (SIADH)
text: Excessively concentrated urine, low blood sodium levels

name: Syndrome of Inappropriate Secretion of ADH (SIADH)
text: Treating underlying cause, fluid restriction, medications to suppress ADH (vasopressin) secretion, addressing electrolyte imbalance

name: Myasthenia Gravis
text: Muscle weakness, fatigue, difficulty speaking

name: Myasthenia Gravis
text: Medications (acetylcholinesterase inhibitors, immunosuppressants), thymectomy (in some cases)

name: Torticollis
text: Neck pain, stiffness, and abnormal head positioning

name: Torticollis
text: Physical therapy exercises, pain relievers, botulinum toxin injections (in severe cases)

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS)
text: Intense burning pain, swelling, changes in skin temperature

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS)
text: Physical therapy, medications, nerve blocks

name: Gastroenteritis (Stomach Flu)
text: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, abdominal pain or cramps, fever

name: Gastroenteritis (Stomach Flu)
text: Rest, fluids (electrolyte solutions), bland diet, avoiding irritating foods, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief

name: Scleritis
text: Eye redness, severe eye pain, blurred vision

name: Scleritis
text: Steroid eye drops, nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), immunosuppressants

name: Cirrhosis
text: Fatigue, jaundice, abdominal swelling (ascites), easy bruising and bleeding, confusion, loss of appetite, weight loss

name: Cirrhosis
text: Treatment aims to manage symptoms, slow down disease progression, and address underlying causes. It may include: lifestyle modifications (e.g., avoiding alcohol, maintaining a healthy diet), medication (e.g., diuretics, lactulose, antiviral drugs), procedures (e.g., paracentesis, transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt), liver transplantation (in severe cases)

name: Lung Contusion
text: Chest pain (sharp or dull), difficulty breathing or shortness of breath, rapid or shallow breathing, coughing up blood or pink-tinged sputum, decreased oxygen levels, wheezing, cyanosis (bluish discoloration of the skin), chest wall bruising, rib fractures (associated injuries)

name: Lung Contusion
text: Supportive care (including supplemental oxygen, pain management, and respiratory support), monitoring in a hospital setting (to assess lung function and overall stability), treatment of associated injuries (such as rib fractures), management of complications (such as pneumonia or respiratory failure), prevention of further complications (such as deep vein thrombosis or pressure ulcers), respiratory therapy (including breathing exercises and airway clearance techniques), possible use of mechanical ventilation in severe cases

name: Corneal Disorder
text: Blurred or hazy vision, eye pain or discomfort, redness or irritation of the eye, excessive tearing, sensitivity to light (photophobia), foreign body sensation, dryness or excessive tearing, corneal abrasions or ulcers, distorted or irregular corneal shape, vision loss

name: Corneal Disorder
text: Treatment depends on the specific corneal disorder and may include: artificial tears or lubricating eye drops, antibiotic or antifungal eye drops (if infection is present), corticosteroid eye drops or ointments (to reduce inflammation), bandage contact lenses, therapeutic contact lenses, corneal transplantation (in severe cases), refractive surgeries (such as LASIK or PRK) to correct vision, protective eyewear or sunglasses, avoiding irritants or triggers, regular eye examinations and monitoring, lifestyle modifications (such as proper eye hygiene and avoiding eye strain)

name: Mucoepidermoid carcinoma
text: Lump, swelling, Difficulty, pain when swallowing and speaking, Facial weakness, Facial paralysis

name: Mucoepidermoid carcinoma
text: Surgery, Radiation therapy

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
text: Tingling, numbness, and pain in the hand and fingers

name: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome
text: Wrist splints, avoiding repetitive hand movements, applying cold or hot compresses, gentle exercises, ergonomic adjustments

name: Macular Degeneration
text: Blurred or distorted central vision, blind spots

name: Macular Degeneration
text: Vision aids, medications (injections into the eye), laser therapy, photodynamic therapy, surgical intervention

name: Atrophic vaginitis
text: Vaginal dryness, Vaginal burning, frequent urination, urinary tract infections, Painful intercourse

name: Atrophic vaginitis
text: Vaginal moisturizers, Vaginal estrogen therapy, Lifestyle modifications

name: Osteochondrosis
text: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling, reduced range of motion

name: Osteochondrosis
text: Physical therapy, pain medications, rest, surgery

name: Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy (CIDP)
text: Weakness, numbness, tingling in limbs

name: Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy (CIDP)
text: Immunoglobulin therapy, corticosteroids, physical therapy

name: Parasitic Infections
text: Intense itching, visible lice or nits in the affected area

name: Parasitic Infections
text: Prescription medications (topical or oral antiparasitic agents), washing clothes and bedding in hot water

name: Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus
text: Gait disturbances, urinary incontinence, cognitive decline or dementia-like symptoms

name: Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus
text: Ventriculoperitoneal shunt placement, medication management for symptoms

name: Kidney Stone
text: Severe pain in the side or back, blood in the urine, frequent urination

name: Kidney Stone
text: Pain management, increased fluid intake, medication to facilitate stone passage, minimally invasive procedures or surgery

name: Pituitary Disorder
text: Various symptoms depending on the specific disorder, including hormonal imbalances

name: Pituitary Disorder
text: Treatment depends on the specific pituitary disorder and may involve medications, surgery, hormone replacement therapy

name: Pinguecula
text: Yellowish or white growth on the conjunctiva (eye's surface), usually near the cornea

name: Pinguecula
text: Artificial tears, lubricating eye drops, sunglasses for UV protection, surgical removal (in severe cases)

name: Influenza (Flu)
text: High fever, body aches, fatigue, cough, sore throat, congestion

name: Influenza (Flu)
text: Rest, fluids, over-the-counter medications for symptom relief, antiviral medications (in some cases)

name: Cat Scratch Disease
text: Swollen lymph nodes, fever, fatigue, headache, rash

name: Cat Scratch Disease
text: Antibiotics (azithromycin, doxycycline), pain management, supportive care

name: Hyperemesis Gravidarum
text: Severe nausea and vomiting during pregnancy

name: Hyperemesis Gravidarum
text: Intravenous fluids to prevent dehydration, anti-nausea medications, nutritional support, rest, monitoring for weight loss and electrolyte imbalance

name: Diabetic retinopathy
text: Blurred or distorted vision, floaters, impaired color vision, dark or empty areas in the visual field, vision loss

name: Diabetic retinopathy
text: Management of blood sugar levels, blood pressure control, cholesterol management, regular eye examinations, laser treatment , intravitreal injections of anti-VEGF medications, vitrectomy

name: Aplastic Anemia
text: Fatigue, pale skin, frequent infections, shortness of breath, rapid or irregular heartbeats, prolonged bleeding or bruising

name: Aplastic Anemia
text: Blood transfusions, bone marrow-stimulating medications, immunosuppressive therapy, stem cell transplant (in severe cases), supportive care

name: Wilson Disease
text: Fatigue, abdominal pain or swelling, jaundice, neurological symptoms (tremors, difficulty speaking or walking)

name: Wilson Disease
text: Medications to remove copper from the body (chelators), dietary changes, liver transplant (in severe cases)

name: Drug Abuse (Barbiturates)
text: Sedation, drowsiness, slurred speech, confusion, respiratory depression

name: Drug Abuse (Barbiturates)
text: Medical detoxification, supportive care, counseling, rehabilitation programs

name: Primary Insomnia
text: Difficulty falling asleep or staying asleep, daytime sleepiness, irritability, difficulty concentrating

name: Primary Insomnia
text: Cognitive-behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I), sleep hygiene practices (such as maintaining a regular sleep schedule, creating a comfortable sleep environment), relaxation techniques, stimulus control techniques (such as limiting bed activities to sleep only), sleep restriction therapy, medications for insomnia (such as sedative-hypnotics, melatonin agonists)

name: Mastoiditis
text: Ear pain (particularly behind the ear), swelling and redness of the ear or the area behind the ear, fever, headache, hearing loss or impaired hearing, drainage of fluid or pus from the ear, earache, irritability (in infants and young children), tenderness or pain when touching the area, mastoid bone protrusion or swelling

name: Mastoiditis
text: Antibiotics (oral or intravenous) to treat the underlying bacterial infection, pain relievers (such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen) for pain and fever, ear drops (if there is drainage from the ear), surgical intervention (in severe or chronic cases), such as mastoidectomy (removal of infected mastoid bone cells) or tympanostomy tube placement (to relieve pressure and drain fluid from the middle ear), supportive care (rest, hydration, warm compresses)

name: Acinic cell carcinoma
text: Lump, swelling, Facial changes

name: Acinic cell carcinoma
text: Surgery, Radiation therapy

name: Arthritis
text: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling

name: Arthritis
text: Medications, physical therapy, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Wharton's duct stones
text: Swelling, pain, dry mouth, bad taste

name: Wharton's duct stones
text: Hydration,Saliva stimulation, Stone removal

name: Pharyngitis
text: Sore throat, difficulty swallowing, swollen lymph nodes

name: Pharyngitis
text: Rest, pain relievers, throat lozenges, antibiotics (if bacterial infection), symptomatic relief

name: Pericarditis
text: Chest pain (sharp and stabbing), worsens with deep breaths or lying down, fever

name: Pericarditis
text: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs) to reduce inflammation and pain, colchicine (for recurrent or persistent pericarditis), corticosteroids (in severe cases or when NSAIDs are ineffective), antibiotics (if bacterial infection is present), lifestyle modifications (rest, avoiding strenuous activity), treating underlying causes (if identified), potential pericardiocentesis (fluid drainage)

name: Antihypertensives Poisoning
text: Dizziness, low blood pressure, slow heart rate, nausea, vomiting

name: Antihypertensives Poisoning
text: Immediate medical attention, activated charcoal administration, supportive care, specific antidotes

name: Hypothyroidism
text: Fatigue, weight gain, sensitivity to cold, dry skin, constipation, depression, muscle weakness, elevated cholesterol levels

name: Hypothyroidism
text: Hormone replacement therapy with synthetic thyroid hormone (levothyroxine), regular monitoring of thyroid hormone levels and adjustment of medication dosage, lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet and regular exercise), management of associated symptoms (such as cholesterol-lowering medications or antidepressants), education and support for long-term management

name: Oral Leukoplakia
text: White patches or plaques in the mouth, often on the tongue or inside of the cheeks, that cannot be scraped off

name: Oral Leukoplakia
text: Regular monitoring and follow-up, identification and elimination of potential irritants (such as tobacco or alcohol), improving oral hygiene practices, medication (such as topical retinoids or antifungal agents), surgical removal (in some cases), cryotherapy (freezing the patches), laser therapy, lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet), regular dental check-ups, education and support

name: Seborrheic Dermatitis
text: Red, itchy, and oily patches of skin, dandruff, flaky scalp

name: Seborrheic Dermatitis
text: Topical antifungal creams or ointments, medicated shampoos, corticosteroid creams

name: Hydrocephalus
text: Headache, nausea, vomiting, cognitive or developmental problems, enlarged head in infants

name: Hydrocephalus
text: Shunt placement (to divert excess cerebrospinal fluid), endoscopic third ventriculostomy, medication (in some cases)

name: Marijuana Abuse
text: Impaired memory and cognition, altered judgment, anxiety

name: Marijuana Abuse
text: Behavioral therapy, counseling, support groups, medication, detoxification

name: Chronic Otitis Media
text: Ear pain, hearing loss, recurrent ear infections

name: Chronic Otitis Media
text: Antibiotics, ear drops, surgical intervention (in some cases)

name: Pterygium
text: Growth on the conjunctiva (white part of the eye)

name: Pterygium
text: Artificial tears, eye lubricants, surgical removal (in severe cases)

name: Hepatic Encephalopathy
text: Confusion, forgetfulness, personality changes, tremors

name: Hepatic Encephalopathy
text: Treating underlying liver disease, medications to manage symptoms, dietary modifications

name: Tietze Syndrome
text: Chest pain, swelling and tenderness of the cartilage connecting the ribs to the breastbone

name: Tietze Syndrome
text: Nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs), pain relievers, local heat or ice application, avoiding activities that worsen symptoms, supportive measures (such as wearing loose clothing or using supportive pillows), physical therapy (to improve posture and strengthen muscles), corticosteroid injections (in some cases), stress reduction techniques

name: Nose Disorder
text: Nasal congestion, runny nose, sneezing, facial pain or pressure, loss of smell

name: Nose Disorder
text: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (such as allergies, sinusitis, or nasal polyps), and may include medications (such as decongestants, antihistamines, nasal sprays), saline nasal irrigation, steam inhalation, allergy management, antibiotics (if bacterial infection is present), surgical intervention (in some cases)

name: Exfoliation Glaucoma
text: Elevated intraocular pressure, visual field loss

name: Exfoliation Glaucoma
text: Eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (e.g., prostaglandin analogs, beta blockers, alpha agonists, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), laser trabeculoplasty, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Protein Deficiency
text: Muscle wasting, fatigue, weakness, delayed wound healing, swelling of the limbs, hair loss, impaired immunity

name: Protein Deficiency
text: Increasing protein intake through diet (lean meats, fish, dairy, legumes, nuts), protein supplements, nutritional counseling, addressing underlying causes (e.g., malabsorption)

name: Thrombocytopenia
text: Easy bruising, prolonged bleeding from cuts, petechiae (red or purple spots on the skin), nosebleeds, excessive menstrual bleeding

name: Thrombocytopenia
text: Treatment of underlying cause, platelet transfusion (if severe), medication (corticosteroids, immunosuppressants), avoiding medications that can worsen platelet counts

name: Alopecia
text: Hair loss or balding, patchy or generalized hair loss

name: Alopecia
text: Topical medications (minoxidil), oral medications (finasteride), steroid injections, hair transplant surgery, wigs or hairpieces

name: Polymyalgia Rheumatica
text: Muscle pain and stiffness, joint pain, fatigue

name: Polymyalgia Rheumatica
text: Low-dose corticosteroids, nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDs)

name: Bone Cancer
text: Bone pain, fractures, fatigue, unexplained weight loss

name: Bone Cancer
text: Surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy

name: Preeclampsia
text: High blood pressure, proteinuria, swelling, headaches, visual disturbances

name: Preeclampsia
text: Bed rest, blood pressure monitoring, frequent prenatal visits, medication (such as antihypertensive drugs), magnesium sulfate (for severe cases), delivery of the baby (in severe cases)

name: Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy (HOCM)
text: Shortness of breath, especially during physical activity or exertion, chest pain or discomfort, dizziness or lightheadedness, fainting or near-fainting episodes, heart palpitations (rapid or irregular heartbeat), fatigue, swelling in the ankles, feet, or legs

name: Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy (HOCM)
text: Medications to relieve symptoms and improve heart function (such as beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers, and disopyramide), medications to prevent abnormal heart rhythms (such as anti-arrhythmic drugs), medications to reduce blood clotting (such as aspirin or anticoagulants), implantable cardioverter-defibrillator (ICD) placement (to monitor and correct abnormal heart rhythms), septal myectomy (surgical removal of a portion of the thickened heart muscle), alcohol septal ablation (destruction of excess heart muscle with alcohol injection), lifestyle modifications (such as avoiding strenuous activities, managing stress, and maintaining a healthy weight), regular follow-up visits with a cardiologist

name: Nonalcoholic Liver Disease (NASH)
text: Fatigue, abdominal swelling or pain, enlarged liver, elevated liver enzymes, insulin resistance

name: Nonalcoholic Liver Disease (NASH)
text: Lifestyle changes (weight loss, healthy diet, regular exercise), management of underlying conditions, medication as needed

name: Amyloidosis
text: Fatigue, weight loss, organ dysfunction

name: Amyloidosis
text: Treatment depends on the type and extent of organ damage

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-3
text: Decreased urine output, swelling in the legs and ankles, and signs of fluid overload, Severe kidney damage

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-3
text: Supportive Measures, Gastric Decontamination, Antidote Administration, Hemodialysis

name: Fracture of the Rib
text: Chest pain, difficulty breathing, tenderness or swelling over the ribcage

name: Fracture of the Rib
text: Pain management, rest, immobilization (splinting or bracing), physical therapy

name: Breast Cancer
text: Breast lump, changes in breast size or shape, nipple changes, breast pain

name: Breast Cancer
text: Surgery (lumpectomy or mastectomy), radiation therapy, chemotherapy, hormone therapy, targeted therapy

name: Neuromyelitis Optica
text: Optic neuritis (vision loss, pain with eye movement), transverse myelitis (weakness, numbness, or paralysis of the limbs, loss of bladder or bowel control)

name: Neuromyelitis Optica
text: High-dose corticosteroids, plasma exchange (to remove antibodies from the blood), immunosuppressive therapy (such as azathioprine or rituximab), supportive care to manage symptoms, physical therapy or rehabilitation for functional recovery

name: Eye alignment disorder
text: Double vision, Eye fatigue, Poor depth perception, Head tilting

name: Eye alignment disorder
text: Glasses, Patching therapy, Vision therapy, Botox injections, Surgical intervention

name: Meniere Disease
text: Vertigo, hearing loss, tinnitus, ear fullness

name: Meniere Disease
text: Medications (diuretics, anti-vertigo drugs), lifestyle changes

name: Pituitary Adenoma
text: Headaches, vision problems (such as blurred vision or loss of peripheral vision), hormonal imbalances (such as excessive production or deficiency of specific hormones), fatigue, weight gain or loss, menstrual irregularities, infertility, erectile dysfunction, growth abnormalities in children, mood changes, cognitive impairments

name: Pituitary Adenoma
text: Observation and monitoring (for small, non-secreting tumors that are not causing significant symptoms), medication (to manage hormonal imbalances or reduce tumor size), surgery (transsphenoidal surgery to remove the tumor), radiation therapy (in cases where surgery is not possible or to treat residual or recurrent tumors), targeted therapy (in cases of aggressive or resistant tumors), hormone replacement therapy (if necessary), regular follow-up and monitoring to assess tumor growth and hormone levels, management of associated complications or hormone deficiencies

name: Ischemia of the Bowel
text: Severe abdominal pain, bloody stools, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, fever, rapid heart rate, low blood pressure

name: Ischemia of the Bowel
text: diagnostic tests, intravenous fluids and medications to stabilize the patient, bowel rest, resection, bypass procedure, potential angioplasty

name: Hydrocele of the Testicle
text: Swelling of the scrotum

name: Hydrocele of the Testicle
text: Observation, surgical drainage (in some cases)

name: Endometrial Cancer
text: Abnormal vaginal bleeding, pelvic pain or discomfort, changes in bowel or bladder habits

name: Endometrial Cancer
text: Surgery (hysterectomy), radiation therapy, chemotherapy, hormone therapy

name: Endometriosis
text: Pelvic pain, painful periods, infertility

name: Endometriosis
text: Pain medications, hormonal therapies, surgery

name: Hydronephrosis
text: Flank pain, urinary frequency, swelling in the abdomen

name: Hydronephrosis
text: Treating underlying cause, medications, ureteral stent, surgery

name: Bell Palsy
text: Facial weakness or paralysis, drooping of the mouth or eyelid

name: Bell Palsy
text: Corticosteroids, antiviral medications, eye protection, physical therapy

name: Abscess of Nose
text: Pain, swelling, redness, discharge from the nose

name: Abscess of Nose
text: Antibiotics, warm compresses, surgical drainage (in severe cases)

name: Bladder Disorder
text: Frequent urination, urgency, pelvic pain

name: Bladder Disorder
text: Medications, bladder training, lifestyle changes

name: Emphysema
text: Shortness of breath, wheezing, chronic cough, chest tightness, fatigue, weight loss

name: Emphysema
text: Smoking cessation (the most important intervention), medications to manage symptoms (such as bronchodilators, inhaled corticosteroids), pulmonary rehabilitation (exercise training, breathing techniques), supplemental oxygen therapy (in advanced cases), vaccination against respiratory infections, avoiding exposure to irritants (such as secondhand smoke or air pollutants), regular monitoring of symptoms and lung function, surgical interventions (such as lung volume reduction surgery or lung transplantation)

name: Extrapyramidal Effect of Drugs
text: Muscle stiffness, tremors, involuntary movements

name: Extrapyramidal Effect of Drugs
text: Adjusting medication dosage, switching to alternative medications

name: Vasculitis
text: Inflammation of blood vessels leading to various symptoms depending on the affected organs, including rash, fever, fatigue, muscle and joint pain, shortness of breath, weight loss

name: Vasculitis
text: Treatment depends on the type and severity of vasculitis. It may include corticosteroids, immunosuppressive medications (such as cyclophosphamide or azathioprine), biologic therapies (such as rituximab), disease-specific treatments (such as plasmapheresis for certain types), supportive care to manage symptoms (such as pain relievers or blood pressure medications), monitoring and management of associated complications (such as organ damage or infections)

name: Female Infertility of Unknown Cause
text: Inability to conceive after one year of unprotected intercourse, irregular or absent menstrual cycles, hormonal imbalances, age-related factors, other

name: Female Infertility of Unknown Cause
text: Fertility testing and evaluation (hormone tests, imaging, genetic screening), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, stress reduction), fertility treatments (assisted reproductive technologies, fertility medications)

name: Syringomyelia
text: Muscle weakness, loss of sensation, chronic pain

name: Syringomyelia
text: Surgical decompression, draining of syrinx (cyst), physical therapy, pain management techniques

name: Von Hippel-Lindau Disease
text: Hemangioblastomas (tumors in the brain, spinal cord, or other organs), retinal angiomas (abnormal blood vessel growth in the retina), renal cell carcinomas (kidney tumors), pheochromocytomas (adrenal gland tumors), pancreatic cysts/tumors, other tumors or cysts

name: Von Hippel-Lindau Disease
text: Regular monitoring and surveillance to detect and manage tumor growth and associated complications, surgical removal or treatment of tumors if necessary, embolization or radiation therapy for certain tumors, medication or surgery to manage hormonal issues (such as pheochromocytomas), genetic counseling and testing, screening and management for other potential complications (such as kidney function monitoring)

name: Hemorrhoids
text: Swollen, itchy, or painful veins in the rectal area

name: Hemorrhoids
text: Increasing fiber intake, staying hydrated, avoiding straining during bowel movements, warm sitz baths, topical creams or ointments, over-the-counter pain relievers (after consulting with a healthcare provider)

name: Bursitis
text: Joint pain, swelling, tenderness, limited range of motion

name: Bursitis
text: Rest, ice, compression, elevation (RICE therapy), pain relievers, physical therapy, steroid injections

name: Cannabis Use Disorder
text: Increased appetite, relaxation, altered perception of time, impaired memory and concentration, bloodshot eyes, anxiety or paranoia (in some individuals), withdrawal symptoms (such as irritability, insomnia) when attempting to stop or reduce cannabis use, continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems, job difficulties)

name: Cannabis Use Disorder
text: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Heart Block
text: Fatigue, dizziness, fainting, chest pain or discomfort, shortness of breath, irregular heart rhythms

name: Heart Block
text: Medications (such as beta-blockers, calcium channel blockers), pacemaker implantation (in severe cases or symptomatic heart block), underlying condition management

name: Hemolytic Anemia
text: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, jaundice

name: Hemolytic Anemia
text: Treatment of underlying cause, blood transfusions, medications (immunosuppressants, corticosteroids), splenectomy (in some cases), supportive care, folic acid or iron supplementation

name: Benzodiazepine Use Disorder
text: Sedation, relaxation, reduced anxiety, drowsiness, poor coordination, memory impairment, withdrawal symptoms (such as rebound anxiety, insomnia, tremors) when attempting to stop or reduce benzodiazepine use, continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems, health risks)

name: Benzodiazepine Use Disorder
text: Gradual tapering off benzodiazepines under medical supervision, counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Mitral Valve Disease
text: Fatigue, shortness of breath, heart palpitations

name: Mitral Valve Disease
text: Medications, lifestyle changes, surgical repair or replacement of the valve (in severe cases)

name: Varicose Veins
text: Enlarged, swollen veins, often in the legs

name: Varicose Veins
text: Elevating legs, wearing compression stockings, regular exercise, avoiding prolonged sitting or standing, maintaining a healthy weight

name: Recurrent salivary stones
text: Frequent episodes of stone formation

name: Recurrent salivary stones
text: Hydration,Saliva stimulation, Stone removal

name: Ocular Hypertension
text: Elevated intraocular pressure without optic nerve damage

name: Ocular Hypertension
text: Observation with regular eye exams, use of eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (if necessary)

name: Colonic Polyp
text: Most colonic polyps do not cause symptoms. Some larger polyps or certain types may cause rectal bleeding or changes in bowel habits, such as diarrhea or constipation.

name: Colonic Polyp
text: Polypectomy (removal of polyp during colonoscopy), surveillance colonoscopy (regular monitoring for recurrence or new polyps), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, smoking cessation), follow-up based on pathology findings

name: Indigestion
text: Abdominal pain, bloating, heartburn, nausea, acidic taste

name: Indigestion
text: Lifestyle changes (e.g., dietary modifications, stress reduction), over-the-counter antacids, medication, therapy

name: Acute Glaucoma
text: Severe eye pain, blurred vision, halos around lights

name: Acute Glaucoma
text: Medications (eye drops), laser therapy, surgery (in some cases)

name: Hypoglycemia
text: Shaking, dizziness, confusion, sweating

name: Hypoglycemia
text: Consuming glucose or sugar-containing foods, adjusting medication or insulin dosage

name: Sciatica
text: Sharp pain, tingling, or numbness that radiates from the lower back through the hips and down the leg

name: Sciatica
text: Rest, gentle stretching exercises, warm or cold compresses, prenatal massages, maintaining good posture, avoiding activities that worsen the pain

name: Type 2 Diabetes
text: Fatigue, Increased hunger, Slow healing of wounds

name: Type 2 Diabetes
text: Oral medications, Insulin therapy (in some cases), Blood sugar monitoring, Exercise

name: Cystic Fibrosis
text: Persistent cough with thick mucus, recurrent lung infections, poor growth or weight gain, salty-tasting skin

name: Cystic Fibrosis
text: Medications (mucus-thinning drugs, bronchodilators), chest physical therapy, enzyme supplements, lung transplant (in severe cases)

name: Insulin Overdose
text: Low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) symptoms (e.g., confusion, sweating)

name: Insulin Overdose
text: Immediate treatment of hypoglycemia, medical assistance, adjusting insulin dosage and regimen

name: Peritonsillar Abscess
text: Severe sore throat, difficulty swallowing, fever, swollen tonsils, voice changes, neck pain or stiffness

name: Peritonsillar Abscess
text: Antibiotics to treat infection, drainage of the abscess (needle aspiration or incision and drainage), pain relievers, warm saltwater gargles, rest, fluids, surgical removal of the tonsils (in chronic or recurrent cases)

name: Leishmaniasis
text: Skin sores, fever, weight loss, enlarged spleen or liver

name: Leishmaniasis
text: Antiparasitic medications, treatment of complications, vector control measures (in endemic areas)

name: Pulmonary Congestion
text: Shortness of breath, cough, wheezing, fatigue, chest discomfort or tightness

name: Pulmonary Congestion
text: Diuretics, medications to manage underlying conditions (e.g., heart failure), oxygen therapy, lifestyle changes

name: Magnesium Deficiency
text: Muscle twitches or cramps, fatigue, weakness, loss of appetite, nausea, vomiting, irritability, mental confusion, irregular heartbeat, tingling or numbness, personality changes, seizures

name: Magnesium Deficiency
text: Dietary changes (consuming magnesium-rich foods), oral magnesium supplements, intravenous magnesium therapy (in severe cases), addressing underlying causes (e.g., malabsorption), medical supervision and monitoring

name: Fracture of the Shoulder
text: Shoulder pain, swelling, limited range of motion

name: Fracture of the Shoulder
text: Immobilization (sling, brace), pain management, physical therapy, surgical intervention (in severe cases)

name: Thrombophlebitis
text: Redness, warmth, swelling, and tenderness along a vein, pain or discomfort, skin discoloration, visible veins, fever

name: Thrombophlebitis
text: Medications to manage pain and inflammation, compression stockings or bandages to promote blood flow, elevation of the affected limb, warm compresses, regular movement and exercise, treatment of underlying causes (such as infection or intravenous catheter removal), anticoagulant therapy to prevent clot formation or progression

name: Encephalitis
text: Headache, fever, confusion, seizures, neurological symptoms

name: Encephalitis
text: Hospitalization, antiviral medications (if caused by a viral infection), supportive care

name: Fat Embolism
text: Respiratory distress, chest pain, confusion, petechial rash (small red or purple spots), neurological symptoms

name: Fat Embolism
text: Supportive care (oxygen therapy, fluid resuscitation), managing symptoms (pain medication, anticoagulants), treatment of underlying cause if possible

name: Cyst of the Eyelid
text: Painful lump or swelling on the eyelid, redness, tenderness

name: Cyst of the Eyelid
text: Warm compresses, good eyelid hygiene, antibiotic ointments (if infected), surgical removal (if necessary)

name: Dislocation of the Patella
text: Knee pain, swelling, inability to straighten the leg

name: Dislocation of the Patella
text: Manual reduction, immobilization, physical therapy

name: Conjunctivitis due to Allergy
text: Redness, itching, watery discharge, swollen eyelids

name: Conjunctivitis due to Allergy
text: Allergen avoidance, antihistamine eye drops, cold compresses, lubricating eye drops

name: Coagulation (Bleeding) Disorder
text: Easy bruising, prolonged bleeding after injury or surgery, excessive bleeding from minor cuts or abrasions, frequent nosebleeds, heavy menstrual bleeding

name: Coagulation (Bleeding) Disorder
text: Blood transfusions, clotting factor replacement therapy, medications to stimulate clotting, lifestyle modifications, surgery (in some cases)

name: Idiopathic Absence of Menstruation
text: Lack of menstrual periods

name: Idiopathic Absence of Menstruation
text: Evaluation to determine underlying cause, hormone therapy

name: Migraine
text: Severe headaches, nausea, sensitivity to light and sound

name: Migraine
text: Pain-relieving medications, lifestyle changes, preventive medications

name: Temporomandibular Joint Disorder
text: Jaw pain or tenderness, clicking or popping sounds in the jaw joint, difficulty chewing or opening the mouth

name: Temporomandibular Joint Disorder
text: Self-care measures (rest, applying heat or cold, jaw exercises), pain medications, physical therapy, oral splints

name: Vitamin A Deficiency
text: Night blindness, dry eyes, dry or rough skin, poor wound healing, weakened immune system

name: Vitamin A Deficiency
text: Vitamin A supplementation, dietary changes to include foods rich in vitamin A

name: Glucocorticoid Deficiency
text: Fatigue, weakness, weight loss, low blood pressure, abdominal pain

name: Glucocorticoid Deficiency
text: Hormone replacement therapy with glucocorticoid medications

name: Astigmatism
text: Blurred or distorted vision, eye strain, headaches,uneven curvature of cornea

name: Astigmatism
text: Eyeglasses, contact lenses, refractive surgery (LASIK, PRK), orthokeratology, toric intraocular lenses

name: Low-Tension Glaucoma
text: Gradual loss of vision, visual field defects

name: Low-Tension Glaucoma
text: Eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (e.g., prostaglandin analogs, beta blockers, alpha agonists, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), oral medications (e.g., carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), laser trabeculoplasty, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Headache after lumbar puncture
text: throbbing headache

name: Headache after lumbar puncture
text: Epidural blood patch, Conservative measures

name: Flat Feet
text: Foot pain or achiness, difficulty with foot and ankle movement, foot fatigue, swelling

name: Flat Feet
text: Supportive footwear, orthotic inserts, physical therapy exercises, pain management, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Carbon Monoxide Poisoning
text: Headache, dizziness, nausea, confusion

name: Carbon Monoxide Poisoning
text: Fresh air, oxygen therapy, immediate medical attention

name: Orbital Cellulitis
text: Eye pain, swelling, redness, vision changes

name: Orbital Cellulitis
text: Antibiotics, hospitalization (in severe cases), surgical drainage (in some cases)

name: Pulmonary Eosinophilia
text: Cough, shortness of breath, wheezing, chest pain, fever, night sweats, fatigue, weight loss, elevated eosinophil count in the blood or sputum

name: Pulmonary Eosinophilia
text: Identification and treatment of underlying causes (such as parasitic infections, drug reactions, or allergic reactions), corticosteroids (such as prednisone) to reduce inflammation, bronchodilators (such as inhalers) to relieve bronchospasm and improve airflow, antiparasitic medications (if parasitic infection is identified), immunosuppressive medications (in severe cases), avoidance of triggers or allergens (if identified), symptomatic treatment for associated symptoms (such as fever or cough), regular monitoring and follow-up with a healthcare professional

name: Antimicrobial Drugs Poisoning
text: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, neurological symptoms

name: Antimicrobial Drugs Poisoning
text: Immediate medical attention, supportive care, antidote administration

name: Eczema
text: Itchy, red, inflamed skin, rash

name: Eczema
text: Emollients (moisturizers), topical corticosteroids or immunomodulators, avoiding triggers

name: Pneumonia
text: Cough, fever, chest pain, difficulty breathing

name: Pneumonia
text: Antibiotics, antiviral medications (if applicable), rest, fluids, symptomatic relief

name: Intestinal Malabsorption
text: Chronic diarrhea, weight loss, abdominal pain or bloating, nutrient deficiencies (vitamins, minerals), fatigue, failure to thrive

name: Intestinal Malabsorption
text: Dietary modifications (specific to the underlying cause), supplementation of deficient nutrients, medications (to manage symptoms or underlying condition), treatment of underlying cause

name: Conductive Hearing Loss
text: Reduced hearing sensitivity, muffled or blocked sound

name: Conductive Hearing Loss
text: Medications, surgical intervention, hearing aids

name: Pinworm Infection
text: Itching around the anus or vagina, restless sleep, irritability, anal or vaginal discharge, abdominal pain or discomfort, loss of appetite

name: Pinworm Infection
text: Anthelmintic medication, hygiene measures (handwashing, trimming nails, laundering bedding), treatment for household members

name: Esophageal Varices
text: Swollen blood vessels in the esophagus, vomiting blood, black, tarry stools

name: Esophageal Varices
text: Medications (beta-blockers, vasoconstrictors), endoscopic therapies, band ligation, transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunt (TIPS), surgical interventions

name: Dermatitis due to Sun Exposure
text: Sunburn-like rash, redness, itching

name: Dermatitis due to Sun Exposure
text: Moisturizers, cool compresses, topical corticosteroids

name: Gonorrhea
text: Painful urination, abnormal discharge from the genitals, pelvic pain

name: Gonorrhea
text: Antibiotics (dual therapy with ceftriaxone and azithromycin), safe sexual practices, partner notification

name: Chronic Sinusitis
text: Facial pain or pressure, nasal congestion, thick nasal discharge, postnasal drip, cough, fatigue

name: Chronic Sinusitis
text: Saline nasal irrigation, nasal corticosteroids, antibiotics (in bacterial cases), decongestants, pain relievers, surgery (in severe or chronic cases)

name: Concussion
text: Headache, dizziness, nausea or vomiting, confusion, memory problems, sensitivity to light or noise, mood changes

name: Concussion
text: Physical and cognitive rest, gradual return to normal activities, symptom management (such as pain relievers or anti-nausea medication), monitoring for complications (such as changes in neurological function), cognitive rehabilitation (if persistent cognitive problems), management of associated symptoms (such as sleep disturbances or mood changes), education and support for recovery, avoiding further head injuries

name: Parathyroid Adenoma
text: Fatigue, weakness, bone pain, frequent urination, kidney stones

name: Parathyroid Adenoma
text: Surgical removal of the adenoma (parathyroidectomy), medications to manage calcium levels

name: Herpangina
text: Sore throat, fever, painful sores or blisters in the mouth and throat, difficulty swallowing, loss of appetite

name: Herpangina
text: Symptomatic relief (pain relievers, throat lozenges, mouth rinses), rest, fluids, maintaining good oral hygiene, avoiding spicy or acidic foods

name: Conversion Disorder
text: Neurological symptoms that cannot be explained by a medical condition or injury, such as paralysis, blindness, seizures, or difficulty speaking

name: Conversion Disorder
text: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or psychodynamic therapy), physical therapy or rehabilitation (to improve physical function and reduce disability), addressing underlying stressors or psychological factors, supportive care and reassurance, potentially medications (such as antidepressants or anti-anxiety medications)

name: Lymphedema
text: Swelling in arms or legs, feeling of heaviness or tightness

name: Lymphedema
text: Compression therapy, exercise, lymphatic drainage techniques, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Hirschsprung Disease
text: Chronic constipation, abdominal distension, failure to pass meconium (in newborns)

name: Hirschsprung Disease
text: Surgery to remove the affected part of the colon (pull-through procedure)

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
text: Joint pain, stiffness, swelling, fatigue, loss of appetite

name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
text: Medications (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs), physical therapy, exercise

name: Turner syndrome
text: Short stature, Gonadal dysgenesis, Webbed neck, Lymphedema

name: Turner syndrome
text: Growth hormone therapy, Estrogen replacement therapy, Cardiac and renal evaluations

name: Sciatica
text: Pain radiating from the lower back to the leg

name: Sciatica
text: Pain medications, physical therapy, stretching exercises

name: Endophthalmitis
text: Eye pain, redness, swelling, blurred vision, decreased vision, floaters (dark spots or cobwebs in the vision), increased sensitivity to light (photophobia), discharge from the eye, excessive tearing, fever

name: Endophthalmitis
text: Immediate medical attention is required. Treatment may include: intravenous antibiotics or antifungal medications, intravitreal injections (injections of antibiotics or antifungal agents directly into the eye), vitrectomy (surgical removal of the gel-like fluid within the eye), topical or oral medications (to manage pain, inflammation, or infection), frequent follow-up visits with an ophthalmologist, supportive care (such as warm compresses or lubricating eye drops)

name: Myelodysplastic Syndrome
text: Fatigue, frequent infections, easy bruising or bleeding

name: Myelodysplastic Syndrome
text: Supportive care, blood transfusions, medications, bone marrow transplantation (in some cases)

name: Premature Ventricular Contractions (PVCs)
text: Skipped or extra heartbeats, palpitations, lightheadedness

name: Premature Ventricular Contractions (PVCs)
text: Monitoring (in most cases), lifestyle changes, medications (beta-blockers, antiarrhythmics)

name: Ludwig's angina
text: Severe swelling of the floor of the mouth, fever, difficulty swallowing, drooling

name: Ludwig's angina
text: Intravenous antibiotics, airway management, surgical drainage

name: Cryptococcosis
text: Headache, fever, fatigue, cough, meningitis, pneumonia

name: Cryptococcosis
text: Antifungal medication (e.g., Amphotericin B, Fluconazole), supportive care

name: Diabetic Kidney Disease
text: High blood pressure, increased need to urinate, swelling in the legs and ankles, fatigue, nausea, loss of appetite

name: Diabetic Kidney Disease
text: Blood pressure control, blood sugar control, medications to protect the kidneys (such as ACE inhibitors or ARBs), lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, quitting smoking), management of underlying diabetes, treatment of complications (such as dialysis or kidney transplantation in advanced stages)

name: Premenstrual Tension Syndrome
text: Mood swings, bloating, breast tenderness, irritability

name: Premenstrual Tension Syndrome
text: Lifestyle changes, medications (pain relievers, hormonal contraceptives), therapy

name: Drug Abuse (Methamphetamine)
text: Increased energy and alertness, decreased appetite, rapid heartbeat

name: Drug Abuse (Methamphetamine)
text: Detoxification, counseling, behavioral therapies, support groups, medication-assisted treatment (if applicable)

name: Urge Incontinence
text: Sudden, intense urge to urinate followed by involuntary urine leakage

name: Urge Incontinence
text: Behavioral therapies (bladder training, pelvic floor exercises), medications (anticholinergics, beta-3 agonists), medical devices

name: Dengue Fever
text: High fever, severe headache, joint and muscle pain, rash, nausea or vomiting, bleeding tendencies

name: Dengue Fever
text: Supportive care (hydration, pain relievers, rest), monitoring, medical attention for severe cases (fluid replacement, blood transfusion, hospitalization)

name: Lead Poisoning
text: Fatigue, abdominal pain, joint pain, memory loss or cognitive difficulties, developmental delays (in children), anemia

name: Lead Poisoning
text: Removal of lead source, chelation therapy (in severe cases), supportive care, medication to manage symptoms, addressing complications (such as anemia)

name: Actinic Keratosis
text: Rough, scaly patches on the skin, usually in sun-exposed areas

name: Actinic Keratosis
text: Topical medications (e.g., fluorouracil, imiquimod, diclofenac), cryotherapy, curettag

name: Choledocholithiasis
text: Abdominal pain (often in the upper right quadrant), jaundice, dark urine, pale stools, nausea, vomiting, endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) with stone removal, sphincterotomy, Cholecystectomy

name: Choledocholithiasis
text: Non-surgical treatment options: Medications to manage pain and discomfort, antibiotics

name: Head and Neck Cancer
text: Persistent sore throat, difficulty swallowing, voice changes, ear pain, neck mass, weight loss

name: Head and Neck Cancer
text: Treatment depends on the specific type and stage of cancer, and may include surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy

name: Sjögren's syndrome
text: Dry mouth and eyes, fatigue, joint pain, dry skin

name: Sjögren's syndrome
text: Symptomatic relief with artificial tears, saliva substitutes, frequent sips of water, medications to stimulate saliva production, immune-modulating drugs

name: Peritonitis
text: Severe abdominal pain, tenderness, bloating, fever, nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite, increased heart rate

name: Peritonitis
text: Intravenous antibiotics to treat the infection, intravenous fluids to restore hydration, drainage or removal of any underlying fluid or abscess, surgery to repair or remove the source of infection

name: Anemia of Chronic Disease
text: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, decreased exercise tolerance

name: Anemia of Chronic Disease
text: Treating the underlying chronic disease, addressing nutritional deficiencies, blood transfusions (in severe cases)

name: Ovarian Torsion
text: Sudden and severe abdominal or pelvic pain on one side, sharp or stabbing pain, nausea, vomiting, fever, rapid heartbeat, abdominal tenderness, bloating, swelling, pain during intercourse, abnormal bleeding or spotting

name: Ovarian Torsion
text: Emergency medical attention (as ovarian torsion is a medical emergency), imaging tests (such as ultrasound or CT scan) to diagnose torsion, surgical intervention (laparoscopic or open surgery) to untwist the ovary and restore blood flow, removal of the ovary (if it is damaged or non-viable), pain management (medications for pain relief)

name: Sciatica
text: Lower back pain, leg pain, numbness or tingling

name: Sciatica
text: Pain relievers, physical therapy, exercise

name: Lupus
text: Joint pain, fatigue, skin rashes

name: Lupus
text: Medications (anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressants), lifestyle modifications

name: Neonatal Jaundice
text: Yellowing of the skin and eyes, pale stools, dark urine

name: Neonatal Jaundice
text: Phototherapy, exchange transfusion (in severe cases), monitoring of bilirubin levels, addressing underlying causes (e.g., breastfeeding)

name: Gastrointestinal Hemorrhage
text: Abdominal pain, vomiting blood, bloody or black stools

name: Gastrointestinal Hemorrhage
text: Blood transfusions, endoscopy, surgery (in severe cases), medications to stop bleeding

name: Dislocation of the Ankle
text: Swelling, severe pain, inability to bear weight

name: Dislocation of the Ankle
text: Reduction (realigning the bones), immobilization, rehab

name: Vaginal Cyst
text: Small lump or swelling in the vaginal wall, discomfort or pain during intercourse

name: Vaginal Cyst
text: Observation (if asymptomatic), warm compresses, sitz baths, surgical removal (if necessary)

name: Orthorexia Nervosa
text: Obsessive focus on "clean" eating, healthy food choices

name: Orthorexia Nervosa
text: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or acceptance and commitment therapy), nutrition education, challenging distorted beliefs, addressing underlying issues

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
text: Frequent urination, burning sensation during urination, cloudy or bloody urine, pelvic pain

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
text: Antibiotics, increased fluid intake, pain relievers

name: Placenta Previa
text: Vaginal bleeding, painless bleeding in the third trimester

name: Placenta Previa
text: Bed rest, avoiding strenuous activity, frequent prenatal check-ups, potential hospitalization, potential cesarean delivery

name: Acute Pancreatitis
text: Severe upper abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, fever, rapid pulse, swollen and tender abdomen, jaundice

name: Acute Pancreatitis
text: Hospitalization, intravenous fluids to restore hydration, pain management, bowel rest, nutritional support, medications to control pain and inflammation

name: Gum Disease
text: Swollen, red, tender gums, bleeding gums

name: Gum Disease
text: Oral hygiene practices (brushing, flossing), professional dental cleanings, medications, surgical interventions

name: Panic Attack
text: Chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations

name: Panic Attack
text: Breathing exercises, relaxation techniques, therapy

name: Lice
text: Itching and visible presence of lice or nits (eggs) in the hair

name: Lice
text: Over-the-counter or prescription medications specifically designed to kill lice (such as pediculicides), manual removal of lice and nits using a fine-toothed comb, washing and drying of infested clothing and bedding at high temperatures, vacuuming of upholstery and household surfaces, disinfection of personal items that cannot be washed or dried, avoiding direct head-to-head contact with infested individuals, education and prevention strategies
