name: Pyloric stenosis
text: Persistent hunger, Weight loss, Dehydration, Visible peristalsis, Palpable mass, Fussiness

name: Pyloric stenosis
text: Ultrasound, Blood tests, Pyloromyotomy

name: Pigmentary Glaucoma
text: Blurred vision, halos around lights, eye pain

name: Pigmentary Glaucoma
text: Eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (e.g., prostaglandin analogs, beta blockers, alpha agonists, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), laser trabeculoplasty, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Abscess
text: Swelling, pain, redness, fever, pus formation

name: Abscess
text: Antibiotics, surgical drainage

name: Myositis
text: Muscle weakness, pain, inflammation

name: Myositis
text: Medications (corticosteroids, immunosuppressants), physical therapy

name: Von Willebrand Disease
text: Easy or excessive bruising, prolonged or excessive bleeding from cuts or injuries, frequent nosebleeds, heavy or prolonged menstrual periods, bleeding gums, blood in urine or stool

name: Von Willebrand Disease
text: Desmopressin (DDAVP) medication, replacement therapy with von Willebrand factor (VWF) concentrate or cryoprecipitate, oral contraceptives (for women with heavy menstrual bleeding), avoiding certain medications, dental care precautions, regular medical follow-up

name: Gastroparesis
text: Nausea, vomiting, early satiety, bloating, abdominal pain, poor appetite

name: Gastroparesis
text: Dietary modifications (small, frequent meals; low-fiber, low-fat diet), medication to promote gastric emptying, managing underlying cause if possible

name: Chancroid
text: Painful ulcers or sores on the genital area, swollen lymph nodes in the groin

name: Chancroid
text: Antibiotics (such as azithromycin or ceftriaxone), pain management, warm compresses, good hygiene

name: Fungal Infection of the Skin
text: Rash, itching, redness, peeling skin

name: Fungal Infection of the Skin
text: Antifungal creams, powders, or oral medications, proper hygiene and skin care

name: Stimulant Use Disorder
text: Increased energy and alertness, euphoria, decreased appetite, increased heart rate and blood pressure, insomnia, restlessness, irritability, paranoia, psychosis (in severe cases)

name: Stimulant Use Disorder
text: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing), support groups, medication (if applicable), addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
text: Extreme fatigue, cognitive difficulties, unrefreshing sleep

name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
text: Symptom management (such as medications to address pain, sleep disturbances, and other symptoms), graded exercise therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, sleep management techniques, pacing and energy conservation, stress reduction techniques, nutritional support, medications for specific symptoms

name: Threatened Pregnancy
text: Vaginal bleeding, abdominal cramps, pelvic pain

name: Threatened Pregnancy
text: Bed rest, medications (progesterone), monitoring, avoiding activities that may stress the pregnancy

name: Chronic Glaucoma
text: Gradual vision loss, peripheral vision impairment, eye pain or discomfort, halos around lights

name: Chronic Glaucoma
text: Eye drops (to reduce intraocular pressure), oral medications, laser therapy, surgery (trabeculectomy, implantable devices), regular eye exams

name: Urticaria (Hives)
text: Raised, itchy welts on the skin

name: Urticaria (Hives)
text: Antihistamines, avoiding triggers, corticosteroids (in severe cases)

name: Vitreous Hemorrhage
text: Floaters in vision, blurry vision, eye pain

name: Vitreous Hemorrhage
text: Observation, laser treatment, vitrectomy (in severe cases)

name: Binge Eating Disorder
text: Recurrent episodes of binge eating without compensatory behaviors

name: Binge Eating Disorder
text: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or interpersonal therapy), nutritional counseling, medications (in some cases), support groups

name: Hypercholesterolemia
text: High levels of cholesterol in the blood, fatty deposits (xanthomas) on the skin or tendons, chest pain or angina, heart attacks, strokes

name: Hypercholesterolemia
text: Lifestyle modifications (healthy diet, regular exercise, weight management, smoking cessation), medications to lower cholesterol levels (such as statins), regular monitoring of cholesterol levels, management of underlying conditions (such as diabetes or high blood pressure), potential additional treatments (such as bile acid sequestrants or PCSK9 inhibitors)

name: Stress Incontinence
text: Unintentional urine leakage during physical activity

name: Stress Incontinence
text: Pelvic floor exercises, behavioral techniques, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Labyrinthitis
text: Vertigo, dizziness, nausea, hearing loss, ear pain

name: Labyrinthitis
text: Medications (antibiotics, antivirals), vestibular rehabilitation therapy

name: Dysthymic Disorder
text: Persistent depressive symptoms (low mood, lack of interest, changes in sleep and appetite), lasting for at least two years

name: Dysthymic Disorder
text: Psychotherapy (cognitive-behavioral therapy, interpersonal therapy), antidepressant medications

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-1
text: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, General malaise, weakness, Increased thirst, frequent urination

name: Ethylene glycol poisoning-1
text: Supportive Measures, Gastric Decontamination, Antidote Administration, Hemodialysis

name: Impulse Control Disorder
text: Impulsive behaviors (e.g., gambling, stealing), difficulty resisting urges

name: Impulse Control Disorder
text: Psychotherapy, medication (in some cases), support groups, behavioral interventions

name: Intracranial Hemorrhage
text: Severe headache, nausea or vomiting, changes in vision, loss of consciousness, seizures

name: Intracranial Hemorrhage
text: Emergency medical attention, surgery (if needed), medications to control bleeding and manage symptoms

name: Postpartum Depression
text: Persistent feelings of sadness, emptiness, or hopelessness, frequent crying, irritability, loss of interest or pleasure in activities, difficulty bonding with the baby, changes in appetite or sleep patterns, overwhelming fatigue, feelings of guilt or worthlessness, trouble concentrating or making decisions, thoughts of self-harm or harming the baby

name: Postpartum Depression
text: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or interpersonal therapy), support groups or peer counseling, antidepressant medication (if recommended by a healthcare professional), hormone therapy (in some cases), lifestyle changes (such as exercise, healthy diet, sleep hygiene), involving a partner or family in the care of the baby, social support and understanding from family and friends, regular follow-up and monitoring by healthcare professionals

name: Injury to the Leg
text: Pain, swelling, bruising, difficulty walking

name: Injury to the Leg
text: R.I.C.E. (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), pain medications, physical therapy

name: Stroke
text: Sudden numbness or weakness of the face, arm, or leg (typically on one side of the body), confusion, trouble speaking or understanding, severe headache

name: Stroke
text: Emergency medical attention, medication (clot-busting drugs, anticoagulants), supportive care, rehabilitation (physical therapy, speech therapy), lifestyle modifications (diet, exercise, smoking cessation), addressing underlying risk factors

name: Huntington Disease
text: Motor, cognitive, and psychiatric symptoms

name: Huntington Disease
text: Supportive care, medications (to manage symptoms), therapy

name: Mononucleosis
text: Fatigue, sore throat, swollen lymph nodes

name: Mononucleosis
text: Rest, pain relievers, fluids, symptom management

name: Hallucinogen Use Disorder
text: Altered perception of reality, visual or auditory hallucinations, changes in sensory experiences, introspective thoughts, feelings of connection or disconnection, anxiety or paranoia (in some individuals), flashbacks (in some individuals)

name: Hallucinogen Use Disorder
text: Counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy, motivational interviewing), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Ependymoma
text: Headaches, nausea, seizures, changes in coordination or balance

name: Ependymoma
text: Surgery, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, targeted therapy, rehabilitation

name: Lymphoma
text: Swollen lymph nodes, unexplained weight loss, fatigue

name: Lymphoma
text: Chemotherapy, radiation therapy, immunotherapy, targeted therapy

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
text: Painful urination, frequent urination, pelvic pain, cloudy urine

name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
text: Antibiotics safe for pregnancy, increased fluid intake, maintaining good hygiene, urine culture and sensitivity testing to guide antibiotic selection

name: Transient ischemic attack
text: Sudden onset of neurological symptoms that resolve within 24 hours

name: Transient ischemic attack
text: Antiplatelet medications, Carotid endarterectomy,  Anticoagulant medications, Lifestyle modifications

name: Normal-Tension Glaucoma
text: Gradual loss of vision, visual field defects

name: Normal-Tension Glaucoma
text: Eye drops to lower intraocular pressure (e.g., prostaglandin analogs, beta blockers, alpha agonists, carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), oral medications (e.g., carbonic anhydrase inhibitors), laser trabeculoplasty, surgical procedures (e.g., trabeculectomy, glaucoma drainage implants)

name: Thyroid Disease
text: Fatigue, weight changes, mood swings, dry skin

name: Thyroid Disease
text: Medications (thyroid hormone replacement, antithyroid drugs), radioactive iodine therapy, surgery (in some cases)

name: Bladder Cancer
text: Blood in urine, frequent urination, pelvic pain

name: Bladder Cancer
text: Surgery, chemotherapy, radiation therapy

name: Fracture of the Arm
text: Pain, swelling, deformity, limited mobility or difficulty moving the arm, bruising

name: Fracture of the Arm
text: Immobilization (cast or splint), pain management, physical therapy, surgery (in complex fractures)

name: Mucocele
text: Painless swelling, usually on the lower lip or floor of the mouth

name: Mucocele
text: Observation, surgical removal if necessary

name: Kidney Failure
text: Decreased urine output, fluid retention, fatigue, shortness of breath, nausea, confusion, chest pain, seizures

name: Kidney Failure
text: Treatment depends on the underlying cause and may include medications to manage blood pressure and symptoms, dietary modifications (restricting salt, protein, and potassium), dialysis (hemodialysis or peritoneal dialysis) or kidney transplantation (in end-stage kidney failure), treatment of complications, management of fluid and electrolyte balance, lifestyle modifications (exercise, quitting smoking, healthy diet)

name: Chagas Disease
text: Fever, fatigue, body aches, heart and digestive problems

name: Chagas Disease
text: Antiparasitic medications, medications to manage symptoms, supportive care

name: Leukemia
text: Fatigue, pale skin, frequent infections, easy bleeding or bruising

name: Leukemia
text: Chemotherapy, radiation therapy, stem cell transplant, targeted therapy, supportive care

name: Vesicoureteral Reflux
text: Frequent urinary tract infections, urinary urgency or frequency, bedwetting (in children), high blood pressure (in severe cases)

name: Vesicoureteral Reflux
text: Antibiotics, bladder training exercises, surgical correction (in some cases)

name: Endometriosis
text: Pelvic pain (during menstruation, intercourse, or bowel movements), heavy menstrual bleeding, infertility

name: Endometriosis
text: Pain management (such as nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs [NSAIDs], hormonal contraceptives, or progestins), hormone therapy (such as gonadotropin-releasing hormone [GnRH] agonists or oral contraceptives), surgical interventions (such as laparoscopic excision or hysterectomy), fertility treatments (if pregnancy is desired), lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, stress management), complementary therapies (such as acupuncture or dietary changes)

name: Psoriasis
text: Red, scaly patches on the skin, itching

name: Psoriasis
text: Topical treatments (corticosteroids, salicylic acid), systemic medications (retinoids, immunosuppressants), phototherapy

name: Infection of Open Wound
text: Redness, swelling, warmth, pain or tenderness at the wound site, pus or drainage from the wound, fever, malaise

name: Infection of Open Wound
text: Cleaning and irrigation of the wound, antibiotic therapy (oral or intravenous), wound dressings, wound debridement (if necessary), tetanus prophylaxis

name: Anemia
text: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath

name: Anemia
text: Iron supplementation, dietary changes (iron-rich foods), managing underlying causes (such as treating iron deficiency or vitamin B12 deficiency), prenatal vitamins

name: Spondylosis
text: Neck or back pain, stiffness, limited range of motion, numbness or weakness in the extremities

name: Spondylosis
text: Medications (pain relievers, muscle relaxants), physical therapy, exercise, posture improvement, heat or cold therapy, spinal injections, surgery (in severe cases or if conservative treatments fail)

name: Acute Otitis Media
text: Ear pain, fever, fluid drainage from the ear, hearing loss or muffled hearing in the affected ear

name: Acute Otitis Media
text: Antibiotics, pain relievers (e.g., acetaminophen, ibuprofen), warm compresses, ear drops, tympanostomy tubes (in some cases)

name: Fracture
text: Pain, Swelling, Bruising, Deformity, Difficulty moving, Loss of function

name: Fracture
text: Immobilization, Surgery, Rehabilitation

name: Round Ligament Pain
text: Sharp or aching pain in the lower abdomen or groin

name: Round Ligament Pain
text: Resting, changing positions slowly, warm compresses, gentle stretching, avoiding sudden movements

name: Autism
text: Challenges with social interaction, communication difficulties, restricted interests or repetitive behaviors

name: Autism
text: Behavioral therapy, educational interventions, speech and language therapy, medications for associated symptoms

name: Pleomorphic adenoma
text: Painless lump or swelling, Facial changes

name: Pleomorphic adenoma
text: Surgical excision

name: Otosclerosis
text: Hearing loss (gradual and progressive), tinnitus (ringing in the ears), dizziness or balance problems

name: Otosclerosis
text: Hearing aids, surgical interventions (such as stapedectomy or stapedotomy to replace the affected bone in the middle ear), sodium fluoride therapy (to slow down the progression of the condition), communication strategies and support (such as lip-reading or sign language), regular hearing assessments

name: Bulimia Nervosa
text: Recurrent episodes of binge eating followed by purging behaviors

name: Bulimia Nervosa
text: Psychotherapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy or dialectical behavior therapy), nutritional counseling, medications (in some cases), support groups

name: Poisoning due to Analgesics
text: Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, liver or kidney damage, dizziness, confusion, respiratory distress

name: Poisoning due to Analgesics
text: Immediate medical attention, activated charcoal administration, supportive care, antidote (if available), treatment for complications

name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism
text: Hypocalcemia (low calcium levels), tetany (muscle spasms), short stature, rounded face

name: Pseudohypoparathyroidism
text: Calcium and vitamin D supplementation, management of complications, hormone replacement therapy (if necessary)

name: Dermatitis due to Sun Exposure
text: Redness, itching, rash, blistering

name: Dermatitis due to Sun Exposure
text: Topical corticosteroids, moisturizers, avoiding sun exposure

name: Anemia due to Malignancy
text: Fatigue, weakness, shortness of breath, pale skin

name: Anemia due to Malignancy
text: Treating underlying malignancy, blood transfusions, medications (erythropoiesis-stimulating agents, iron supplements)

name: Hyperhidrosis
text: Excessive sweating (beyond what is necessary for temperature regulation), typically in specific areas (such as palms, soles, underarms), may interfere with daily activities

name: Hyperhidrosis
text: Antiperspirants, medications (anticholinergics, beta blockers), iontophoresis, Botox injections, surgical interventions (sympathectomy, sweat gland removal)

name: Otitis Media
text: Ear pain, fever, fluid drainage from the ear

name: Otitis Media
text: Antibiotics (if bacterial infection), pain relievers, observation (in some cases)

name: Benign Kidney Cyst
text: Abdominal or flank pain, blood in urine, frequent urination

name: Benign Kidney Cyst
text: Observation, medication (if needed), surgery (in rare cases)

name: Pain after an Operation
text: Surgical site pain, discomfort, swelling

name: Pain after an Operation
text: Pain medications, ice packs, rest, wound care

name: Chronic Knee Pain
text: Persistent knee pain, stiffness, swelling, limited range of motion

name: Chronic Knee Pain
text: Pain management, physical therapy, lifestyle modifications, knee braces or supports

name: Volvulus
text: Abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, bloating, constipation, bloody stool

name: Volvulus
text: Emergency surgery to untwist the affected organ and restore blood flow

name: Scurvy
text: Fatigue, swollen and bleeding gums, joint pain

name: Scurvy
text: Vitamin C supplementation, dietary changes, oral hygiene maintenance

name: Urinary Stones (Kidney Stones)
text: Severe abdominal or back pain, blood in urine, frequent urination, pain during urination

name: Urinary Stones (Kidney Stones)
text: Pain management, increased fluid intake, medications to help pass the stones, procedures or surgery

name: Injury to the Finger
text: Pain, swelling, bruising, deformity, restricted range of motion, difficulty gripping or using the finger

name: Injury to the Finger
text: RICE method (Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation), splinting, pain medication, physical therapy, surgery (in severe cases)

name: Retinal Detachment
text: Floaters (spots or cobweb-like shapes in vision), flashes of light, curtain-like shadow over vision, sudden vision loss

name: Retinal Detachment
text: Surgical intervention (pneumatic retinopexy, scleral buckle, vitrectomy), laser therapy, cryotherapy, gas or silicone oil injection, positioning, regular eye exams

name: Chronic Rheumatic Fever
text: Joint pain, fever, rash, heart inflammation

name: Chronic Rheumatic Fever
text: Antibiotics (to treat streptococcal infections), anti-inflammatory medications, medications for heart complications

name: Acute Bronchospasm
text: Wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness

name: Acute Bronchospasm
text: Bronchodilators (inhaled medications), corticosteroids, supplemental oxygen (if needed)

name: Vulvodynia
text: Persistent pain or discomfort in the vulva, burning, stinging, rawness, itching

name: Vulvodynia
text: Topical medications (such as lidocaine or estrogen creams), nerve blocks, physical therapy (including pelvic floor muscle relaxation exercises), cognitive-behavioral therapy (CBT), biofeedback, medications (such as tricyclic antidepressants or anticonvulsants), vulvar care and hygiene practices, stress management techniques, alternative therapies (such as acupuncture or herbal remedies)

name: Other Specified Feeding or Eating Disorder (OSFED)
text: Symptoms that do not meet the full criteria for a specific eating disorder

name: Other Specified Feeding or Eating Disorder (OSFED)
text: Treatment is based on the specific symptoms and can include a combination of psychotherapy, nutritional counseling, medical monitoring, and medications as necessary

name: Anemia due to Chronic Kidney Disease
text: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin

name: Anemia due to Chronic Kidney Disease
text: Erythropoiesis-stimulating agents (ESA), iron supplementation, blood transfusions (in severe cases)

name: Pemphigus
text: Painful blisters on the skin and mucous membranes

name: Pemphigus
text: Corticosteroids, immunosuppressants, wound care

name: Syphilis
text: Primary stage: painless sores (chancre) at the site of infection, secondary stage: skin rash, fever, swollen lymph nodes, sore throat, latent stage: no symptoms, tertiary stage: severe complications affecting the heart, brain, and other organs

name: Syphilis
text: Antibiotics (usually penicillin), treatment of complications, regular follow-up and testing

name: Omphalitis
text: Redness, swelling, discharge, foul odor around the umbilical area

name: Omphalitis
text: Antibiotics, wound care, surgical intervention (in severe cases)

name: Idiopathic Nonmenstrual Bleeding
text: Abnormal vaginal bleeding outside of the menstrual period, unpredictable bleeding episodes, bleeding after sexual intercourse, bleeding after menopause, spotting or light bleeding between periods, prolonged or heavy bleeding

name: Idiopathic Nonmenstrual Bleeding
text: Evaluation to rule out underlying causes (such as hormonal imbalances, polyps, infections, or uterine abnormalities), hormonal therapy (such as birth control pills, hormone replacement therapy), non-hormonal medications (such as tranexamic acid), dilation and curettage (if necessary to remove excessive tissue or investigate the cause), endometrial ablation (in some cases), hysteroscopy (to examine the uterus), surgery (in rare cases), counseling or support for emotional well-being

name: Myofascial Pain Syndrome
text: Localized muscle pain, trigger points

name: Myofascial Pain Syndrome
text: Physical therapy, trigger point injections, dry needling, medications (such as pain relievers, muscle relaxants), relaxation techniques, stress management, massage therapy, myofascial release, heat or cold therapy

name: Vocal cord polyp
text: Hoarseness, Vocal Changes, Vocal Fatigue

name: Vocal cord polyp
text: Voice Rest, Speech Therapy, Surgical Removal

name: Rotator Cuff Injury
text: Shoulder pain, weakness or loss of strength, limited range of motion, shoulder stiffness

name: Rotator Cuff Injury
text: Non-surgical treatment options: Rest, ice or heat therapy, pain medications, physical therapy exercises, Arthroscopic surgery

name: Fibromyalgia
text: Widespread musculoskeletal pain, fatigue, sleep disturbances, cognitive difficulties (fibro fog), mood changes

name: Fibromyalgia
text: Multidisciplinary approach: Medications for pain management (such as analgesics, antidepressants, and anticonvulsants), physical therapy, occupational therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, relaxation techniques, stress management, exercise programs

name: Hypercalcemia
text: Fatigue, nausea, excessive thirst, kidney stones

name: Hypercalcemia
text: Treating underlying cause, hydration, medications

name: Secondary Glaucoma
text: Varies depending on the underlying cause

name: Secondary Glaucoma
text: Treatment of the underlying condition (e.g., medication adjustments, surgery, trauma management, tumor removal, inflammation control)

name: Drug Reaction
text: Rash, itching, swelling, fever, respiratory symptoms

name: Drug Reaction
text: Discontinuation of the offending drug, supportive care, antihistamines, corticosteroids (in severe cases)

name: Acute Sinusitis
text: Facial pain or pressure, nasal congestion, thick nasal discharge, post-nasal drip, cough, headache, fatigue, fever (in some cases)

name: Acute Sinusitis
text: Home remedies (such as nasal saline irrigation, steam inhalation), over-the-counter pain relievers (such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen), nasal decongestants (for short-term use), nasal corticosteroids (to reduce inflammation), plenty of fluids and rest, warm compresses, avoidance of irritants (such as smoke or allergens), prescription antibiotics (if bacterial infection is suspected or confirmed), management of underlying causes (such as allergies or nasal polyps), symptom relief medications (such as cough suppressants or antihistamines)

name: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome
text: Irregular periods, excessive hair growth, acne, weight gain, ovarian cysts

name: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome
text: Lifestyle changes (diet, exercise, weight management), hormonal contraceptives, fertility treatments (if needed)

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS)
text: Severe and persistent pain, changes in skin color or temperature, swelling, stiffness

name: Complex Regional Pain Syndrome (CRPS)
text: Medications (such as pain relievers, corticosteroids, anticonvulsants), physical therapy, sympathetic nerve blocks, spinal cord stimulation, occupational therapy, mirror therapy, psychotherapy, graded motor imagery, stress management techniques

name: Optic Neuritis
text: Vision loss or blurry vision, eye pain with eye movement, color vision impairment, flashing lights or floaters

name: Optic Neuritis
text: High-dose intravenous corticosteroids, treatment of underlying cause, symptom management, visual rehabilitation

name: Adrenal Cancer
text: Abdominal or back pain, unexplained weight loss, hormonal imbalances

name: Adrenal Cancer
text: Surgery (adrenalectomy), chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, hormone replacement therapy

name: Intracranial Abscess
text: Headache, fever, neurological deficits

name: Intracranial Abscess
text: Antibiotics, surgical drainage or removal of the abscess, supportive care

name: Adenoid cystic carcinoma
text: Lump, swelling, Facial numbness, tingling

name: Adenoid cystic carcinoma
text: Surgery, Radiation therapy

name: Fracture of the Patella
text: Severe pain, swelling, bruising, difficulty straightening or bending the knee, deformity

name: Fracture of the Patella
text: Immobilization (splint, cast, or brace), pain management, physical therapy, exercises to restore mobility and strength, potential surgical intervention (in severe cases or if displacement is present)

name: Restless Leg Syndrome
text: Uncomfortable sensation in the legs, usually in the evening or at night, urge to move legs for relief

name: Restless Leg Syndrome
text: Lifestyle changes (such as regular exercise, avoiding triggers like caffeine), good sleep hygiene (maintaining a regular sleep schedule), leg massages or stretching exercises, hot or cold packs, medication therapy (such as dopamine agonists, anticonvulsants, opioids), iron supplementation (if iron deficiency is present)

name: Cold Sore
text: Small, painful blisters on the lips or around the mouth

name: Cold Sore
text: Antiviral medications (topical or oral), pain relievers, antiviral creams, self-care measures

name: Chronic Kidney Disease
text: Fatigue, swelling of the legs or ankles, decreased appetite, difficulty concentrating, increased urination or urine changes, blood in urine, high blood pressure

name: Chronic Kidney Disease
text: Management of underlying conditions (such as diabetes or high blood pressure), lifestyle modifications (such as healthy diet, regular exercise, smoking cessation), medications (such as ACE inhibitors or diuretics), dialysis (in advanced stages), kidney transplant (in end-stage kidney disease), management of associated complications (such as anemia or bone disease), regular monitoring and follow-up with a nephrologist, education and support for self-care

name: Goiter
text: Enlarged thyroid gland in the neck, swelling or visible protrusion, difficulty swallowing or breathing

name: Goiter
text: Treatment depends on the underlying cause (medication, radioactive iodine therapy, surgery)

name: Type 1 Diabetes
text: Frequent urination, Increased thirst, Weight loss

name: Type 1 Diabetes
text: Insulin therapy, Blood sugar monitoring, Healthy eating, Regular exercise

name: Dislocation of the Elbow
text: Severe pain, visible deformity, swelling, inability to move the elbow, numbness or tingling in the hand/arm

name: Dislocation of the Elbow
text: Closed reduction (realigning the bones without surgery), immobilization (splint or cast), physical therapy, pain management, surgical intervention (in complex or recurrent cases)

name: Rabies
text: Flu-like symptoms (fever, headache, fatigue), anxiety, confusion, hallucinations, difficulty swallowing

name: Rabies
text: Immediate medical attention (post-exposure prophylaxis), wound care, administration of rabies vaccine and immunoglobulin, supportive care, monitoring and management of symptoms

name: Contact Dermatitis
text: Itchy, red rash caused by contact with irritants or allergens

name: Contact Dermatitis
text: Avoiding the trigger, corticosteroid creams or ointments, antihistamines

name: Hemophilia
text: Excessive bleeding and bruising, prolonged bleeding from wounds or after dental work or surgery, spontaneous bleeding in joints or muscles

name: Hemophilia
text: Replacement therapy with clotting factor concentrates, medications to control bleeding, physical therapy, joint protection measures, genetic counseling and testing for family members

name: Open Wound from Surgical Incision
text: Redness, swelling, discharge from the wound

name: Open Wound from Surgical Incision
text: Wound care (cleaning, dressing changes), antibiotics (if infection is present), surgical interventions

name: Chronic Migraine
text: Recurrent, disabling migraines

name: Chronic Migraine
text: Medications (such as preventive medications, pain relievers, triptans), lifestyle modifications (such as identifying triggers, stress management, regular sleep patterns), relaxation techniques, biofeedback, cognitive-behavioral therapy, nerve blocks, Botox injections, neuromodulation devices, acupuncture

name: Hyperkalemia
text: Muscle weakness or fatigue, irregular heartbeat or palpitations, numbness or tingling, nausea or vomiting

name: Hyperkalemia
text: Treatment of underlying cause, medications to reduce potassium levels, dietary modifications, dialysis (in severe cases)

name: Fibromyalgia
text: Widespread musculoskeletal pain, fatigue, sleep disturbances

name: Fibromyalgia
text: Medications (such as pain relievers, antidepressants, anticonvulsants), physical therapy, cognitive-behavioral therapy, stress management techniques, exercise, relaxation techniques, good sleep hygiene

name: Bipolar Disorder
text: Mood swings, manic episodes, depressive episodes

name: Bipolar Disorder
text: Medications (mood stabilizers, antipsychotics), therapy

name: Sedatives Poisoning
text: Drowsiness, confusion, slowed breathing

name: Sedatives Poisoning
text: Immediate medical attention, supportive care, antidote administration

name: Migraine
text: headache, nausea, sensitivity to light, visual disturbances

name: Migraine
text: pain relief medication, rest, cold compress

name: Plantar Fasciitis
text: Heel pain, tenderness, difficulty walking

name: Plantar Fasciitis
text: Rest, stretching exercises, orthotic devices, physiotherapy

name: Priapism
text: Persistent, painful erection

name: Priapism
text: Medical intervention (aspiration, medication, surgery)

name: Gestational Hypertension
text: High blood pressure without proteinuria (preeclampsia)

name: Gestational Hypertension
text: Monitoring blood pressure, lifestyle modifications (such as salt restriction, regular exercise), medication (such as antihypertensive drugs) if necessary, close monitoring by healthcare provider

name: Pyelonephritis
text: Fever, chills, back or flank pain, frequent urination

name: Pyelonephritis
text: Antibiotics (such as fluoroquinolones, cephalosporins, or trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole) to target the specific bacteria causing the infection, adequate hydration, pain management (such as over-the-counter pain relievers or prescription medications), hospitalization (for severe cases or complications), urine culture to guide antibiotic therapy

name: Drug Abuse (Cocaine)
text: Euphoria, increased energy, dilated pupils, rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, risk of heart attack or stroke

name: Drug Abuse (Cocaine)
text: Medical detoxification, counseling, behavioral therapies, support groups

name: Gout
text: Intense joint pain, swelling, redness

name: Gout
text: Medications (nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, colchicine), lifestyle changes

name: Narcolepsy
text: Excessive daytime sleepiness, sudden loss of muscle tone (cataplexy)

name: Narcolepsy
text: Stimulant medications, antidepressants, lifestyle modifications, scheduled naps

name: Submandibular stones
text: Swelling, pain, dry mouth, bad taste

name: Submandibular stones
text: Hydration,Saliva stimulation, Stone removal

name: Dental Caries
text: Toothache, tooth sensitivity, visible pits or holes in the teeth, dark spots on the teeth, bad breath

name: Dental Caries
text: Dental fillings, crowns, root canal treatment, tooth extraction (in severe cases), oral hygiene practices (brushing and flossing), regular dental check-ups, fluoride treatments, dietary modifications (reducing sugary and acidic foods and drinks), dental sealants (for prevention), education on proper oral care

name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
text: Fatigue, weakness, pale skin, shortness of breath, tingling or numbness in the hands and feet, balance problems, sore tongue

name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
text: Vitamin B12 supplementation (such as oral supplements or injections), dietary changes to include B12-rich foods (such as meat, fish, eggs, and dairy products), identification and management of underlying causes (such as pernicious anemia or malabsorption disorders), management of associated symptoms or complications (such as nerve pain or balance problems), regular monitoring of B12 levels and treatment adjustment

name: Testicular Cancer
text: Lump or swelling in the testicles, pain or discomfort, changes in testicular size or shape

name: Testicular Cancer
text: Surgery (orchiectomy), chemotherapy, radiation therapy, surveillance (for some cases)

name: Peyronie Disease
text: Curvature of the penis, pain during erections

name: Peyronie Disease
text: Medications, traction therapy, surgery

name: Hepatitis due to a Toxin
text: Fatigue, jaundice, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting

name: Hepatitis due to a Toxin
text: Supportive care, removal or avoidance of the toxic substance, management of symptoms

name: Foreign Body in the Throat
text: Sensation of a foreign object stuck in the throat, difficulty swallowing or speaking

name: Foreign Body in the Throat
text: Immediate medical attention, removal of the foreign body, evaluation for any complications

name: Stenosis of the Tear Duct
text: Excessive tearing, eye discharge, recurrent eye infections

name: Stenosis of the Tear Duct
text: Tear duct probing, dilation, surgical correction

name: Lateral Epicondylitis (Tennis Elbow)
text: Pain or tenderness on the outer side of the elbow, weak grip strength, difficulty with forearm movements

name: Lateral Epicondylitis (Tennis Elbow)
text: Rest, ice or cold therapy, compression, elbow brace or strap, pain relievers, physical therapy exercises, corticosteroid injections (in severe cases), ergonomic modifications

name: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
text: Inattention, hyperactivity, impulsivity

name: Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD)
text: Behavioral therapy, parent training programs, medication (such as stimulants or non-stimulants), individualized education plans (IEPs) for academic support, accommodations and modifications in school, psychoeducation, counseling

name: Tuberous Sclerosis
text: Benign tumors in various organs, seizures, developmental delays, skin abnormalities

name: Tuberous Sclerosis
text: Medications (anti-seizure, behavioral, etc.), surgery (for tumor removal), therapy (physical, occupational, speech), educational interventions, support services, genetic counseling, monitoring of organ involvement

name: Pulmonic Valve Disease
text: Fatigue, shortness of breath, chest pain or discomfort, heart palpitations, fainting or lightheadedness, swollen legs or ankles

name: Pulmonic Valve Disease
text: Medications (such as diuretics, beta-blockers, or calcium channel blockers), surgical interventions (such as balloon valvuloplasty or valve replacement), lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, heart-healthy diet, smoking cessation), regular follow-up with a cardiologist

name: Pelvic Organ Prolapse
text: Pelvic pressure or heaviness, urinary incontinence, bowel movement difficulties, discomfort or pain during intercourse

name: Pelvic Organ Prolapse
text: Pelvic floor exercises, pessary use, hormone therapy, surgical repair (in severe cases)

name: Sedative, Hypnotic, or Anxiolytic Use Disorder
text: Sedation, relaxation, reduced anxiety, drowsiness, poor coordination, memory impairment, withdrawal symptoms (such as rebound anxiety, insomnia, tremors) when attempting to stop or reduce use, continued use despite negative consequences (such as relationship problems, health risks)

name: Sedative, Hypnotic, or Anxiolytic Use Disorder
text: Gradual tapering off sedatives, hypnotics, or anxiolytics under medical supervision, counseling or therapy (such as cognitive-behavioral therapy), support groups, addressing underlying psychological or social factors, lifestyle changes, addressing co-occurring mental health conditions, harm reduction strategies, ongoing monitoring and support

name: Atrial Fibrillation
text: Irregular or rapid heartbeat, palpitations, shortness of breath, chest pain or discomfort, dizziness or lightheadedness, fatigue

name: Atrial Fibrillation
text: Medications (antiarrhythmics, anticoagulants), electrical cardioversion, catheter ablation, lifestyle changes

name: Asthma
text: Recurrent episodes of wheezing, shortness of breath, chest tightness, coughing, especially at night or in response to triggers such as allergens or exercise

name: Asthma
text: Long-term control medications (such as inhaled corticosteroids, long-acting beta-agonists), quick-relief medications (such as short-acting beta-agonists), avoidance of triggers (such as allergens, smoke), regular monitoring of symptoms and lung function, development of an asthma action plan, proper inhaler technique, regular follow-up with a healthcare provider, emergency medications (such as oral corticosteroids) as needed

name: Edward Syndrome
text: Developmental delays, intellectual disability, distinct facial features (small jaw, low-set ears), heart defects

name: Edward Syndrome
text: Supportive care, addressing associated medical issues, early intervention programs, palliative care

name: West Nile Virus
text: Fever, headache, body aches, rash

name: West Nile Virus
text: Supportive care, symptom management, prevention of mosquito bites

name: Obesity
text: Excessive body weight, increased risk of chronic conditions

name: Obesity
text: Antifungal medication eg  Amphotericin B, Fluconazole, supportive care

name: Hypertension
text: headache, dizziness, blurred vision

name: Hypertension
text: medication, diet change, exercise

name: Sialolithiasis
text: Swelling, pain, tenderness, difficulty eating

name: Sialolithiasis
text: Warm compresses, Saliva stimulation, Sialagogues, Stone removal

name: Hyperopia
text: Difficulty seeing objects up close, blurred vision when focusing on near objects

name: Hyperopia
text: Eyeglasses, contact lenses, refractive surgery

name: Liver Cancer
text: Abdominal pain, jaundice (yellowing of the skin and eyes), unexplained weight loss, loss of appetite, fatigue, nausea, vomiting

name: Liver Cancer
text: Surgery, Radiation therapy, Chemotherapy, Targeted therapy, Immunotherapy, Radiofrequency ablation, Transarterial chemoembolization (TACE), Selective internal radiation therapy (SIRT), Palliative care, Clinical trials

name: Chalazion
text: Painful, red lump or swelling on the eyelid, usually on the upper eyelid, sensitivity to light, blurred vision (if the chalazion affects the line of sight), mild discomfort, tenderness, localized inflammation and swelling

name: Chalazion
text: Warm compresses (to help soften and promote drainage of the blocked oil gland), gentle massage of the affected area, eyelid hygiene (keeping the area clean), over-the-counter lubricating eye drops, topical antibiotic ointments (if there is a risk of infection), corticosteroid injections (for larger or persistent chalazia), surgical drainage or excision (in rare cases or if conservative treatments are ineffective)

name: Hypocalcemia
text: Numbness or tingling in fingers or around the mouth, muscle cramps

name: Hypocalcemia
text: Calcium and vitamin D supplements, treating underlying causes, intravenous calcium (in severe cases)

name: Viral Warts
text: Small, rough growths on the skin or mucous membranes, typically painless, may appear in clusters or as single lesions

name: Viral Warts
text: Topical treatments (salicylic acid, cryotherapy), laser therapy, surgical removal, immune therapy (for persistent or recurrent cases)

name: Spondylitis
text: Chronic back pain and stiffness, especially in the morning or after periods of inactivity, limited range of motion in the spine, fatigue, inflammation and pain in other joints (such as hips, shoulders, knees), discomfort or pain in the neck, difficulty maintaining proper posture, tenderness or swelling in affected areas, tingling or numbness in extremities in severe cases

name: Spondylitis
text: Medication (such as nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs, corticosteroids, disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs), physical therapy (including stretching and strengthening exercises), heat or cold therapy, posture correction techniques, lifestyle modifications (such as regular exercise, weight management), assistive devices (such as braces or supports), surgical intervention (in rare cases), pain management techniques (such as acupuncture or transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation)
